      "content": "How to create a survey? There are 3 ways you can create a survey using QuestionPro: Create a survey from scratch in your account Use a pre designed survey template Upload a survey using a microsoft word document The goal of creating a survey is to elicit holistic information from a target audience that provides enough data points to generate actionable insights. How do I create a survey? To create a survey from scratch, follow these simple steps: Go to: Login » My Surveys. Click on the new survey button. There are two ways of creating a survey: Create a survey from scratch: Click new survey » enter the survey name of your choice » click on the create survey button. Your survey will be created and you can begin adding question types that you prefer, and customize their settings in accordance to your survey goals. Use a template to create a survey: Click new survey » use a template » select the survey template of your choice. A pop-up will appear giving a preview of the selected template. Click on use this template button to create a survey using the selected survey template. If you like, you can customize the questions or answer options within the template. Upload a survey using a word document Click new survey » enter the survey name » click on the create survey button » click on Add questions from MS word. A pop-up will appear giving you the option to upload a word file or download a sample document. Click on Sample Microsoft Word Document button to download a sample file for reference. Once your file is ready for upload, drag and drop the file and click on upload to create your survey. Note: The ‘Add from Microsoft Word’ option is available only when creating a new survey. Once questions are added manually, this option will no longer be accessible. <span>Inline frames not supported</span> Which is the most recommended survey creation method? When you are sure about what kind of survey you wish to create, you have a set goal and the questionnaire to gather relevant data, the ‘new survey’ method is the most recommended as it gives you the ability to design your survey exactly the way you want it to be. When you are not sure about what kind of survey you wish to create or the right questions to ask your target audience, you could always use the ‘templates’ and then edit/customize them accordingly. What's the best way of getting started with QuestionPro? Our weekly training sessions! We conduct a live weekly webinar series which is absolutely FREE!. Each webinar is about 45 minutes long. The topics covered over a four week span include: An overview of QuestionPro An overview of the smart survey logic Survey distribution and reporting Feature updates - What's new with QuestionPro To learn more about these or register yourself for these training sessions, visit our Training page! I have created my survey, what are the next steps? Brand your survey. Add your company's logo, update colors and font Distribute your survey via Email, Sms, QR codes, Offline App or check out the entire list of distribution methods Analyze survey results in real time Related QuestionPro Certifications Survey Design Research Survey Fundamentals License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "steps": [],
      "content": "Add questions Once you have created your survey, you need to add questions as per your requirements. QuestionPro allows you to add questions to your survey from a pre-defined set of questions. How do I add questions to a survey? To add questions to your survey: Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit » Workspace. Click on Add Question button. You can select the questions from the categories mentioned as follows: Basic Advanced The added question will appear in your survey edit page and you can add/update the question text and answer option text. You can also add questions from the library or by uploading MS Word template. How do I delete questions from a survey? Deleting single question: Hover or click on a question that you want to delete. Click on the three dots and select the Delete option. A pop-up will appear to confirm whether you want to proceed further to delete the question. Click on delete question to delete the question permanently. Deleting multiple questions: Go to: Tools » Review Mode. Select the questions you want to delete. Click on the Delete icon. A pop-up will appear to confirm whether you want to proceed further to delete the question. Click on Delete Question to delete the question permanently. Delete action permanently deletes the question and all associated data. Please double check before deleting questions with data. If you delete a question and you have set up branching/logic based on the question, branching/logic will not work after you delete the question. Will the questions be deleted if I delete a block? Yes. When you delete a block, all questions within the block will be deleted. Is there any limit to number of questions I can add to a survey? Yes. For Essentials and Advanced licenses there is a limit of 100 questions that you can add to a survey, with the Team Edition and Research Edition license you can add up to 300 questions to a survey. With QuestionPro you can customize the appearance of your surveys to align them with the look and feel of your brand. In addition to our customization features, you can use any of our standard themes. Each of them was designed to perfectly fit your brand regardless of browser or device. How can I change the look and feel of my survey? Go to: Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit » Design Here you can select from the professionally designed themes under different survey layouts How can I exactly match colors with my brand colors? You can select the color using the color picker or you can enter the HEX value for the color. For more information on color codes, please visit: HTML Colors - w3schools.com Once you have copied the color code, click on the Custom Color theme to open the color picker and paste the hex code for your color and save. How can I change the default theme of my survey? To change the default theme of your Survey: Go to: Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit » Design Click on the Wrench Manage Themes icon. Select the default theme from the dropdown. Once the default theme has been changed, any new survey that you create will have the selected theme set as default.       "id": "branching-skip-logic",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Survey logic",
      "summary": "This QuestionPro help file helps understand and use the branching or skip logic feature that gives you the ability to create “intelligent” surveys, meaning respondents can answer relevant questions based on their answers to screening questions.",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/branching-skip-logic.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:35:24.916Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Branching or skip logic on a question"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Branching or skip logic on a question Branching or skip logic gives you the ability to create “intelligent” surveys that allow respondents to answer selected/relevant questions based on their answers to the screening questions. Skip logic interrupts the default survey flow and jumps/redirects the respondent to another location. This location can be another question, another survey, thank you page, or simply terminate the survey. How do I add branching or skip logic to a question? Before adding any logic, you should setup the entire survey with all the necessary questions. This will make it easier to test and verify the intended logic for the survey. Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit. Select the question you wish to apply logic to and click on logic. On the popup, skip logic will be selected by default. For each answer option, you can set the If selected, Jump to question logic. If you wish, you can update the Piping Text and also assign it to a Custom Variable. Piping text can be used on the following questions. Assigning the piping text to a variable will allow you to use it throughout the rest of the survey and also with reporting. Click here to learn more about piping text. Setting up Default Branching is optional. Default Branching logic will be executed in case none of the branching options are selected. Click the Save Logic button to complete the setup. When logic is added for a question, a page break is automatically inserted. This page break is necessary for branching to function properly and cannot be removed. What are the different jump to options? For each answer option, respondents can be directed or skipped to the following Jump To options: Survey Questions: You can select from any of the questions that are after the source question. Terminate Survey: Select this option if you wish to terminate the survey response. Respondents will be directed to the terminate survey page and the response will be marked as a Terminated response. Thank You page: Select this option if you wish to direct respondents to the Thank You page. Response will be marked as a completed response. Chain Survey: Select this option to take respondents to a different survey. Related QuestionPro Certifications Survey Design Research Survey Fundamentals License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "id": "send-email-invitation",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Sending survey via email",
      "summary": "How to e-mail a survey using the Survey Analytics Enterprise Research Platform.",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/send-email-invitation.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:35:51.915Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Sending survey invitations via email"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Sending survey invitations via email One of the most popular ways to distribute surveys to potential respondents is through an email invitation. Email invitations are highly customizable and they give you the ability to create templates that can be used for a specific survey or across all the surveys. How can I send a survey via email invitation? To send an email invitation: Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Distribute » Email >> Compose In the \"To\" section, you can either add email addresses or select an email list that you have created from the drop-down as shown below: Survey invitations are survey-specific. Please make sure you have selected the correct survey. The active survey name is always displayed in the top left corner. How can I change the email template? The email body/message that you see is a default template which is provided for each survey. You can create your own email templates (survey specific and global) and use them while sending out the survey. To change the email template, click on the default template drop-down. Here, you can either create a new template or select an existing email template that you have created from survey specific templates. You can also select email templates from the global list based on your need. You can also edit the following: Email body: You can edit the email body either in plain text, images or use HTML to customize the look and feel. From email: You can update the email address of the sender. From name: You can update the name of the sender Insert survey link: Select from either the tracked respondent link (will appear as SURVEY_LINK= ) or the anonymous respondent link (will appear as ANONYMOUS_SURVEY_LINK= ). This is also where you can update the survey link text that will be displayed when a respondent sees the email. By default, the survey link text is \"start survey\". Insert variables: Insert first name, last name or custom variables from 1 to 5 using point and click. Language: If your survey is multilingual you can select a default language, so the respondents will receive the survey directly in one language. Email embed: You can embed the first question of your survey in the email body itself. Check how to embed questions Once you are done with the editing, click on the send button to send out the email invitation for your survey. Can I schedule an email invitation? Yes, you can schedule survey invitations by clicking on the schedule button where you can define the future date and time when the invitation has to be sent out. Check how to schedule survey invitations via email. Links in the email are not clickable and the survey cannot be opened. If respondents have received the email but are not able to click on the survey link, check if the email is marked as SPAM or junk. Move the email out of the SPAM/Junk folder and the links should be clickable and respondents should be able to access the survey. Some email clients may remove the hyperlink if the from email address is not part of their safe sender list. Ask them to add the email address as a safe sender. Some clients may remove the hyperlink and display the entire URL in plain text. Respondents can copy this URL to open the survey. If emails are getting caught in SPAM filters, asking recipients to mark your emails as safe improves the inbox delivery rate for emails.       "id": "manage-email-list",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Manage email list",
      "summary": "Create and manage email list",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/manage-email-list.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:35:57.915Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Create and manage email list"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Create and manage email list Sending surveys within QuestionPro’s email distribution tools requires you to create, upload, and manage email lists. You can accomplish this by manually adding contacts or uploading an external file (CSV format) containing contact information. When it’s time to send email invitations, simply select the desired email list and update your email invitation to send out your survey. To manage your email list, you can delete, edit, or add contacts to the existing email lists. Create and manage the global and/or survey-specific email lists. How do I create an email list? To create email lists, Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Distribute » Email. Click on the Lists tab. Here you can see all your email lists and create new ones. Click on the New List button. Enter the list name. By default, the list will be survey specific. You can change to a global list if you want to access this list on other surveys. Click on the Create List button. A new email list will be created. Now, you can upload email addresses in this list. What are the list management options? Under the Lists page, you can click on the More menu (3 dots) to access options to manage your email lists. The following list management options are available: Delete Click on the delete icon to delete an email list. Deleting an email list will invalidate all tracking links sent out. Rename Click to rename your list. Bulk Unsubscribe Click to open the popup where you can unsubscribe emails in bulk. Automated Import Tool You can use this tool to upload and send invitations remotely by emailing a unique email address with the list attached. Export Batch Click to export the survey invitation links for external distribution. Data Sync Use this option to update/edit your email lists or associated custom variables. Email Filter Use this option to filter emails when sending to this list. Email Template Setting How do I add email addresses in the email list? There are two methods to do so: Method 1 -Import Contacts: Step 1: Create an Excel sheet with all the email addresses and data associated with it. Step 2: Upload it in the system. Step 3: Skip the header (optional) [You need to do this if the first row in the sheet is only headers and does not contain any value]. Step 4: Map the values from the dropdown. So the system knows which value means what. Method 2 - Add Contacts (Manual Entry): With this method, you can add email addresses and the data manually in the email list. This method can be used when you have a small email list. Step 1: Click on Add Contacts Manually. Step 2: Select the respective column headers from the drop-down. Step 3: Add the values below it. Step 4: Click Upload. You can also copy and paste email addresses Step 1: Click on Copy / Paste email addresses. Step 2: Select a predefined format. Step 3: Enter the values based on the format. Step 4: Click on Upload. How many email addresses can I upload in one email list? We recommend uploading up to 20,000 email addresses per email list, to add more email addresses please create a new email list.       "content": "Share survey on social media - Facebook, Twitter, and LinkedIn QuestionPro provides a simple one-click solution to post and share your surveys on social media /social networking sites such as Facebook, Twitter, and LinkedIn. You can post the survey as a plain URL that users can click on and navigate to your survey page to respond to the survey. This allows you to collect responses and feedback from a wide variety of audiences for your surveys where you do not want to specify your respondent base. How do I share a survey on social media? To share a survey on social media: Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Distribute. Click on share. Under the share tab, you will see icons for Facebook, Twitter, and LinkedIn. Click on any of the social media icons, for example, Twitter. A pop-up will appear to post your survey on your Twitter account. Click on the tweet button to share your survey. Can I copy survey URL and share it on social media? Yes. To copy and share survey URL on social media: Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey). Copy the survey URL shown in the red box in the below image. Login to your social media account (Twitter). Paste survey URL on your Twitter account and click on tweet. Your survey will be posted on your Twitter wall. Can I customize the look and feel of the post on my social media page? No. Currently, there is no option to customize the look and feel of your survey while posting it on your social media page.       "id": "response-viewer",

      "title": "Responses - raw data",
      "summary": " Responses - raw data ",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/response-viewer.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:36:10.916Z",

        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Responses - raw data"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [
        "Conjoint",
        "Max-Diff",
        "Complex Grid/ Flex Matrix",
        "Heatmap",
        "HotSpot",
        "Custom Logic Engine",
        "Lookup Table",

      ],
      "content": "Responses - raw data Responses that are collected for your surveys are available to view in the raw (unprocessed) data format in the response viewer. It's a real time view of your data. Use the response viewer to view, edit, or delete individual responses. You can also use the response viewer to access individual spotlight reports, email individual responses, print individual responses, or delete responses. Where can I see the responses collected for my survey? Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Analytics Select the responses option from the drop down under dashboard. All your responses are available in the Response Viewer Following options are available in the response viewer: By default, question and answer data is not displayed. To see this data, switch on the toggle switch for Display Questions. You can control which data you want to see by using the Show/Hide Columns option. You can export the raw data by clicking on the XLS icon. You can search for responses in the search bar. You can search using the response id's. You can search multiple response id's using a comma separated list. You can also search using an email address. You can search using either the external reference variable or custom variables 1-5. By default, all responses are displayed under the response viewer. To view responses by particular status, you can click open the All Responses drop-down and select from the following options to view: All responses (default selection) Completed Started but not completed Terminates Data segments - all user created data segments Can I search for specific responses? Yes. You can search the response viewer for responses using either the response ID, email address, external reference variable, or custom variables 1-5. To search for a specific response, if you know the response ID or the email address, you can search by entering this data. You can also search by response ID for multiple response ID's separated by a comma. For email address, external reference variable, or custom variable 1-5; comma separated search for multiple search terms is not possible. Please use a specific search term. Can I view, edit, download individual responses? Yes. You can click on the response id to open the response in a popup. You can edit the response or view the spotlight report here. Following download and export options are available for the individual response. PDF: You can download the individual report in PDF format. Excel: Download the individual response in Excel. Email: You can email the individual report. Print: You can print the individual report. How can I update custom variables in bulk? You can update the External Reference variable along with custom variables 1 - 5 in bulk. Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Analytics Select the Responses option from the drop down under dashboard. Select the responses you wish to update the variables for. Click on update variables. Enter the text with which you wish to update the variables. You can update the External Reference variable along with custom variables 1 to 5. Save the changes. Important notes about editing responses. If your survey has looping - there will be multiple responses generated for the same questions - you cannot use the response editor if your survey has complex looping logic or extraction. Rank order and constant sum validation: When editing responses directly, the system does not validate any of the validation options. Branching logic / skip logic: None of the branching logic or skip patterns are applied when editing responses directly. If you have branching/skip logic, please make sure that you do not enter in data that cannot be validated or is inconsistent with the branching logic that you have setup. Can I delete a single response or specific responses? Yes. Select the response or responses you wish to delete and click on the delete option that is displayed upon selection. Responses once deleted cannot be retrieved. Can I show or hide columns in the response viewer? Yes, you can choose which data is important to you and only view and download that particular data points using the Show/Hide Columns option. On the popup, you can select which data points you want to see. Questions mentioned below are not shown in response viewer, hence won't be displayed in edit mode: Conjoint Max-Diff Complex Grid/ Flex Matrix Heatmap HotSpot Custom Logic Engine Lookup Table Randomizer Store Locator Text Marker Presentation Text TubePulse Question mentioned below are shown in response viewer but are not editable, hence will not be displayed in edit mode: Attach/Upload File Signature Flex Matrix Reference Data Multi Tier Lookup Table License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "content": "Data segmentation and filtering analysis Data segmentation allows you to filter your survey responses by using data filter. You can filter data based on certain parameters such as survey status, date filter, question, custom variables, geo location, email list code, device type, and language. How can I create data filters? Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Analytics » Analysis » Data Filters. The following page will appear: Click on new data filter. A pop-up will appear where you can create and apply a filter. Enter a name for the new filter. Select options for the following parameters to filter out the data. Response status: All, completed, started but not completed, terminated or over-quota. Date filter: You can set a start date and end date to filter out the responses within the date range. Filter criteria: You can set \"if condition\" on question, system variable, geo location, email list code, device type, and language. For example, if you want to set filter criteria for female respondents only, then set if condition as follows: In the first drop-down, select \"Question\". In the second drop-down, select the question on which you want to apply filter criteria - \"Gender\". In the third drop-down, select the operator from the drop-down - \"is\". In the fourth drop-down, select the answer option - \"female\". Click on save filter. Your criteria will now be listed under current data filters and will be available anywhere that the option data set filter appears (Trend Analysis, TURF Analysis, etc.). The data filters will be displayed in the list as follows: The list of data filters can be viewed in detailed or compact view. You can select these options from the drop-down next to detailed view as shown in the above image. If you select compact view, then the question on which filter condition is applied will not be displayed. You can change the response status, add a time filter (date filter) to your data filters and go to the response viewer by clicking the count mentioned under responses (19). You can also perform a few more operations with the help of the following icons displayed in the red box: The first icon is to view the filtered report on the dashboard. The second icon is to view the text report. The third, fourth and fifth icons are for downloading reports in Excel, PPT and Doc formats respectively. The sixth icon is to delete the data filter. However, depending on your license level, there is a limitation on the total number of filters. License level Filters Essentials 1 Advanced 10 Team 15 Research 30 How can I add multiple filters to the same group? Click on +. Select the logical operator (and/or). Now again select another question, choose the operator and then choose the answer option. After adding the filters, click on save filter. System variable / Geo Location This data segment type allows you to choose custom variables or system variables to filter data. The variable names should match the names used in the survey or in the email list that you uploaded. If you want to use a custom variable assigned to slot 6 or higher, use the Extended Variables section, enter the variable number in the Index field, then the value assigned to that custom variable in the Value field. You can also use the geo location filter. Select city, state/county, or country code, then enter the value desired in geo location value. If you want to filter to a specific email list, choose the list from the drop-down provided. How can I share a report for the created data segment? To share a report for the created data segment, go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Analytics. Click on the view more icon. Select the saved grouping in the filter data drop-down Select the data segment filter you saved. A permalink will be generated that you can use to share your data segment dashboard. Do I need to set up the filters before people start taking the survey? NO! You can create the data filters anytime during your data collection. It is independent of the data collection process. Can I use this data filter functionality in combination with advanced analysis tools like Trend and TURF Analysis? Yes! The Trend and TURF modules are integrated with the data filters. I have created the filter, but I don't see any count for the created segment. What have I done wrong? This might happen if the page is cached on your browser. Please refresh the Create Data Filters page after you have created the segments. Make sure that you set the correct operator for the filter (Equals/ Greater than/ Less than, etc.). All the matched responses are included in the segment. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "content": "Cross-tab reports The QuestionPro cross-tabulation tool allows you to measure the interaction between two questions (variables). The table will only show respondents that answered both of the questions, meaning the frequencies shown may differ from a standard frequency table. The cross-tab report will also show Pearson’s Chi-Square Statistics, which shows the level of correlation between the variables using the chi-square, p-value, and degrees of freedom. Click to download video How do I access cross-tabulation? Go to: My Surveys (Select Survey) » Analytics Select Cross-Tabulation from under the Analytics drop-down menu. Click on the cross-tabulation report to open the report or you can create a new report. You can share this report using the Report Link. You can also download this report in XLS or PDF formats. PDF report export is disabled if the cross-tab table width exceeds 1800 px in width. How do I select questions to analyze and apply filters? Click on the settings icon for your cross-tabulation report. Here, you can update your row and column options. Select the questions you wish to add as rows. Update your report. Similarly, you can also select the questions to display as columns. To apply filters, click on the Filters tab under the report settings. You can apply multiple filters along with the following options: Filter based on responses to questions Response status based filters Custom variable filters Pre-defined filters for your data What are the display and statistical analysis options available for the cross-tabulation report? The following display options are available: Count: Displays the count for the cross-tabulation report Row Percentage: Displays the row percentage for the cross-tabulation report Column Percentage: Displays the column percentage for the cross-tabulation report Column Percentage (Base N): Displays the column percentage (Base N) for the cross-tabulation report Overall: Displays the overall column for the cross-tabulation report Row Mean: Displays the row mean Column Mean: Displays the column mean The following analysis options are available with the Research license: Statistical analysis License Heatmap Rows Research Edition Heatmap Columns Research Edition Pearson's Chi Square Research Edition Column Proportion Test Research Edition Fisher's Exact Test Research Edition Cross-Tabulation: How to make actionable business decisions <span>Inline frames not supported</span> License This feature is available with the following licenses : Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful? Select the output file format as Microsoft Excel or CSV What different options are available for downloading? Single Header Row:The default file has 2 header rows to display additional information associated with some question types. Enable this option to merge the row headers into one single row header. Open-Ended Text Data: When enabled Open-Ended Text Data is included in the file. This option is enabled by default. Raw Data: A separate Raw Data sheet is included in the Excel file. Unselected Checkboxes with '0': By default, selected checkboxes are represented by 1, unselected or hidden check boxes are blank. Show questions not displayed: Include questions in the export that were not shown to the respondents. Report labels: Display report labels instead of the question text in the export file. Content url(Upload question data): Exclude the upload question data from the export file. Geo Code & Additional Info: Collect geo code data for the survey if required. You would need to toggle this filter on. Data Filter - Date: You can filter the report based on date, so data for only a particular time period is included. Data Filter - Response Status: You can download data based on the Response Status. Started But Not Completed: Users who have clicked on the \"Continue\" button but have not completed the survey so far. Completed: Users who have completed the survey. Terminates If you have branching logic for termination (screen outs) these responses are flagged as terminated. Excel file showing Raw Data and Individual Question Data CSV file showing Raw Data If responses are more than 5000, we split them into multiple sheets and export a zip file. Genie assisting in searching relevant information from the Help Center. Genie assisting in navigating to the search query of the user. What does Genie look like? Genie appears as a chathead-like bubble on the screen. When clicked, a draggable chatbox opens up for users to type their messages or speak their questions to get answers. Where can I find Genie? Genie is available on every page of QuestionPro. It stays on the page while users interact with the software, making it easy for them to follow instructions conveniently. Can we drag Genie and the chat box? Yes, Genie and the chat box both are draggable.Genie bubble will be at right or left of the screen only while chatbox can be placed anywhere on the screen. What is the purpose of Genie? The purpose of Genie is to assist users with any questions they may have about using the QuestionPro product. Does Genie support different languages Yes, Genie support different languages. For example: If you enter the question in Spanish. then you will get the response in Spanish. How does Genie work? Genie uses artificial intelligence to understand users' questions, whether typed or spoken, and provide relevant answers. Users can interact with Genie by typing their queries into the chatbox and clicking the send button, or by using the microphone button to speak their questions. The microphone button transcribes speech into text, allowing users to review and edit their query before sending it. Genie also offers a speak feature, which lets users listen to the bot's responses. To use the microphone for continuous listening, users need to hold the microphone button while speaking. Can Genie provide assistance with specific features of QuestionPro? Yes, Genie can provide assistance with specific features of QuestionPro. Users can ask questions about any aspect of the product, and Genie will provide relevant information and guidance. How to upload or add files to the media library? To upload images or files go to: Login » Survey » Edit Under the Edit tab, click on Media Library You can select a folder to upload your image to or also add a new folder Once you have selected or created a folder, you can click to add or drag and drop files anywhere on the page. The files will be uploaded and can now be used across the platform. Files uploaded to media library are publicly accessible. Please do not upload any private or sensitive files to the media library. Click here to learn more about encrypted media URLs. The following file types can be uploaded. Types Extension License Level Images GIF, JPG, JPEG, PNG, BMP, WEBP All Paid Audio MP3, WAV Advanced and above Video M4V, M4A, MP4, MOV, AVI, WEBM Advanced and above Documents / Other TXT, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX,PPT, PPTX, PDF Advanced and above Fonts TTF, OTF, WOFF, WOFF2 Research Edition The following are the storage limits License Level Storage Limit Essentials 100 MB Advanced 1 GB Team Edition* 5 GB Research Edition* 5 GB * Shared across all users within the organization. Please contact your account manager to add more storage space. What is the maximum size limit for an individual file that can be uploaded? 100MB. We allow individual files up to 100MB in size. For larger files, you can upload them on any external platform like google drive, dropbox, etc and use the public link for the file on your surveys if needed. Smaller the file size, faster it will load! How to add an image on a survey question? You can add an image on your survey questions or answer options. Click on the text and you can click on the image icon. On the popup, you can either upload a new image or select from an image from your media library. The image will be added to your survey question or answer option as selected. What options are available for the images and other files? Image/file options can be accessed by clicking on the more (vertical ellipsis) icon. The following options are available: View Details: This will open the image preview with all file details. Get Link: Click on the get link option and the image/file URL will be copied to the clipboard. Get HTML: Clicking on get html will copy the HTML for the image/file to clipboard. You can use this on the email invitation or on the survey to display or link files. Set as Logo: Click on this option to use the image as logo for the survey. Rename: Use this option to rename the file. Download: Download the file by clicking on this option. Delete: Delete the file. Can I move files to a different folder? Yes. Under Media Library, make sure you are in the list view. Select the files you wish to move and click on the Move option. Select the folder where you want to move the files. Can I share my media library folders with others within the organization? Yes. You can share your folder with anyone within your organization. Click on the Share Folder option for the folder. On the popup, you can select who you want to share with. Only specific users or teams, or Everyone within your organization Save your sharing preferences What is the common files folder? The common files folder is a system folder which is shared by default with the entire organization. All files added to this folder will be shared with everyone by default. All users can add files to this folder, however, only the organization admin can delete files from this folder. Organization admins can disable the common files folder by switching off the toggle for 'Share with everyone'. Folder options To manage folders, you can click on the kebab (three vertical dots) menu to access options for the folder. Rename: Rename the folder Delete: Delete the folder Set as default: Set the folder as the default folder. This will open the set folder whenever you access the media library or when adding/uploading images. I don't see the option to delete my files. If the file is moved to a shared folder, it cannot be deleted. Please move it out of a shared folder and then you can delete the file. Or you can ask your organization admin to delete files. You will not be able to delete files added by other users. Please ask the organization admin or the user who uploaded the file to delete it.       "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "How to edit live surveys",
      "summary": "How to delete, add and edit questions and answers from a live survey",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/editing-live-survey.html",

      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Deleting / Editing Questions / Answers in a live Survey - Implications and Issues"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Deleting / Editing Questions / Answers in a live Survey - Implications and Issues Can I edit the survey question(s) / answer(s) once the survey is live? Yes, it is possible to edit the survey question(s) / answer(s) after the survey is live. You can change/re-word the text for question(s) / answer(s) by clicking on the Edit link for the concerned question under the Edit Survey page. Can I delete question(s) from a live Survey? Yes, you can delete question(s) from a live Survey. To delete a question from your survey, click on the ellipsis on the top-right corner of the question Please note that deleting the Question will also delete all responses collected for that question. Once a Question is deleted the responses cannot be restored In the pop-up, click on the Delete Question button Can I add question(s) to a live Survey? Yes, Question(s) can be added to a live survey. Data for that question will be collected only from the point onwards after which it was added to the Survey. Can answer options be deleted / adjusted for live Surveys? It is possible to delete / adjust answer options on a live survey however we strongly advise not to do so unless absolutely necessary. For example Q: Which city do you live in? New York Los Angeles Chicago Philadelphia You have a live Survey with the above question and have already collected some data. In the database the data is scored according to the answer options position in the list. So responses will be stored with scores / weights as follows: New York will be recorded as 1 Los Angeles will be recorded as 2 Chicago will be recorded as 3 and Philadelphia will be recorded as 4. Now, if you add another option, Phoenix at position two in the list. The Scoring for the rest of the options will change. So now the scores will be as follows: New York will be recorded as 1 (same as earlier) Phoenix will be recorded as 2 (new answer option added) Los Angeles will be recorded as 3 (earlier saved as 2) Chicago will be recorded as 4 (earlier saved as 3) and Philadelphia will be recorded as 5 (earlier saved as 4). So for a respondent who answered the survey before the changes were made and had answered Chicago as the city i.e. score 3 would now show up under Los Angeles. The data saved before the answer option was added will be useless for the above question. Therefore if absolutely necessary please add the answer option at the end of the list so that the earlier responses are not disturbed. Similarly if an answer option is deleted from the middle of the list the scoring for the rest of the answer options is disturbed and the data that was saved prior to the changes were made will be inconsistent. The survey does not work correctly after I edit the Survey? Please check if you have set up branching in the survey. If you have branching and you added / removed question(s) / answer(s) then you will need to set up the branching again and also set up page breaks appropriately for the survey to work correctly. Also if you were pointing to a question using branching and now that question has been deleted then you will need to update the branching accordingly. Can questions and/or answer options on a live survey be re-ordered? Yes. However, re-ordering questions will affect the flow of the Survey. Therefore if you have branching in the Survey then the branching will need to be updated wherever necessary. Reordering of questions and/or answer options will affect your collected data. Changes can be made to a live survey however we strongly suggest against it. Please do so only if it is absolutely necessary and at your own risk. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it?       "content": "Survey Blocks A survey block is a group of questions within the survey. Survey blocks simplify the way questions are ordered and presented to survey takers. They enable the survey creator to display a set of questions or sections of surveys randomly. Options available for every block Following are the options available for every block: Preview : Use this option to preview a specific block. Reorder : Use this option to reorder the blocks within a survey. Question Randomization : Use this option to randomize the questions within a block. Delete : Use this option to delete the block. Preview a survey block Click on the three dots to the right of the survey block. Click on ‘Preview.’ With this option, you can preview all questions in a specific block. Reorder blocks within a survey To re-order blocks within the survey follow the steps below: Click on the Reorder option on any block. In the pop-up, drag and drop the blocks according to the requirement. Click on Reorder Blocks. This will reorder the blocks within your survey. Split a block into two parts Go to the question from where you want to split the block and at the bottom of that question, you'll find the Split Block button. Click on the Split Block button and it will divide the block into two blocks. Splitting a block will reset the question randomization within the block. Can I merge two blocks into one block? Yes. You can merge two blocks into one by just clicking the Merge Blocks button in between two blocks. Merging two blocks will reset the question randomization within both the blocks. Rename a survey block Click on the block's name and edit. You can also extend and collapse a block by clicking on the top left icon on the block. Delete a survey block Select the block, access the block settings and click on ‘Delete.’ Deleting a block will also delete all questions in that block.       "content": "Block randomizer What is block randomizer? To understand the block randomizer, you have to know what a survey block is. A survey block is a group of questions within the survey. Survey blocks simplify the way questions are ordered and presented to survey takers. They enable the survey creator to display a set of questions randomly. The block randomizer helps to present the survey blocks in random order when a respondent reaches a specified section of the survey. This is useful when you need to randomly show a question from a group of questions or randomly show different sections of a survey to gather a variety of responses. This help file will show you how to add, merge, and split blocks, as well as how to randomize questions within a block, and reorder blocks within the survey. Setting up survey blocks Without any survey questions, you will see the survey template blocks where you can add or merge them together. Create a survey block: Create a new survey. A survey block will be created automatically in the new survey. All questions will be added to this default block. To create a new block, click on ‘Add Block’. Preview a survey block: Click on the three dots to the right of the survey block. Click on ‘Preview.’ With this option, you can preview all questions in a specific block. Reorder a survey block: To reorder blocks within the survey, click on the three dots and select ‘Reorder.’ Drag and drop blocks in preferred order and click on ‘Reorder Blocks’. Display questions randomly in a survey using a block To display questions randomly to different respondents, access the three dots to the right of the survey block and select ‘Question Randomization’. Select questions under the current display order. Use the up/down arrows or left/right arrows to change the order of the questions. Click on Save. Learn more: Question Randomization Delete a survey block Select the block, access the block settings and click on ‘Delete’. Split a survey block into two: You can split one block of survey questions into two. To split the block, click on Split Block at the bottom of the question from where you want to create a new block. It will divide the existing block into two blocks. Splitting a block will reset the question randomization within the block. Merge two or more survey blocks Merging two survey blocks will combine all the questions in those blocks. Merging two blocks will reset the question randomization within both the blocks. Rename a survey block Click on the block’s name and edit it. To expand and collapse survey blocks, click on the top-left icon. Block randomization: Go to your survey. Click on ‘Tools’. Click on ‘Block Flow’ from the drop-down menu. Click on ‘Add Randomizer’ to add a section for the randomizer. Drag and drop the blocks in the section and select the number of blocks you want to randomly display to the respondents. Click on Save. The order decided under block randomizer will be the default order of your survey. Reset block randomizer: To reset the survey block randomizer, delete the randomizers added for that survey. Go to the survey and click on the Tools button. Click on ‘Block Randomizer’. Click on 'Delete' on the top-right position of the randomizer. Can I see which blocks were displayed and in which order for each respondent? Yes. All randomization data is saved for each respondent and can be downloaded in Excel/CSV format. Go to: My Surveys(Select Survey) » Analytics » Manage Data » Export Here, switch on the toggle for Include Randomization Data In the exported file, block order data and question order data will be included. <span>Inline frames not supported</span> License This feature is available with the following license : Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "steps": [

        "Do Not Display : To hide the question(s) which you have placed under this option."
      ],
      "content": "Question Randomization Overview Randomization is a research technique which is used to overcome order bias- the bias that can result due to the order of answer choices. Respondents tend to favour choices at the beginning and at the end of a list because they are more memorized. First items may also be chosen by respondents to save time. Order bias can be took into account by randomizing the order in which the answer choices are presented. How to randomize questions within a block? Follow the steps below to randomize the questions within a section: Click on Question Randomization under block options. In the pop-up, you can see all the questions present under Current Display Order category, which will always be displayed in the same order as presented in the list. You can use the up and down arrows to the left, to reorder the selected question You can use the left and right arrows in front of the respective randomization option to add the selected question into those category. Finally, click on the Save button to save the question randomization. To test the randomization set up you can use the preview option for that block. Randomization Categories There are three randomization categories: Random : To randomly display all the questions you have placed under this option. Random Subset : To display limited questions from the list of questions you have placed under this option. Do Not Display : To hide the question(s) which you have placed under this option.       "id": "survey-blocks-monadic-testing",

      "title": "What is monadic testing?",
      "summary": "This is the detailed help docuemntaiton to set up a monadic test in your QuestionPro aacount.",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/survey-blocks-monadic-testing.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:37:48.915Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "What is monadic testing?"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "What is monadic testing? What is monadic testing? Link Copied In a monadic test, the target audience is broken down into multiple groups. Each group is presented with one of the concepts and asked questions about it. These questions ask the respondents' opinions about the concept and which specific attributes and features they like or dislike. How can I set up monadic test in QuestionPro? Link Copied To set up a monadic test in your QuestionPro account, please follow the steps below: Go to: Surveys >> Edit Add all the questions related to the concepts in separate blocks. Click on the Block Flow option from the Tools drop-down menu, located at the top-right corner of the window. In the pop-up, you'll get the list of blocks added in your survey. Now click on any one of the Add Randomizer link to add a randomizer section. In the pop-up, drag and drop all the blocks within the newly added randomizer section. Select the number 1 in the Randomly display drop-down menu and click on the Save button. Evenly present blocks logic toggle has be deprecated as of September 30th, 2024. You have successfuly created a monadic test survey which will display only 1 concept/respondent to get their response. Pros and cons to monadic testing Link Copied Pros: The respondents are shown only one concept in isolation, independent of any external stimuli. Thus the respondent's opinions about the concept are not impaired by any distractions or outside influences. The data collected is clean and can be used to validate the concept without any constraints. Since the respondents are shown only one concept, you can get in-depth insights by asking more follow up questions while still keeping the survey relatively short. Cons: Since the target audience needs to be broken down into multiple groups, you need a larger sample size, thus increasing your research cost. It becomes even more challenging when you want to research a niche market, and it isn't easy to find enough respondents to create multiple groups.         {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "What is sequential monadic testing?"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "What is sequential monadic testing? What is sequential monadic testing? Link Copied In a sequential monadic test, respondents are presented with two or more concepts in an isolated environment. The concepts are presented in a random order to avoid order bias. Each concept is followed by the same series of questions to determine in-depth insights about each of the concepts. How can I set up a sequential monadic test in QuestionPro? Link Copied To set up a sequential monadic test in your QuestionPro account, please follow the steps below: Go to: Surveys » Edit Add all the questions related to the concepts in separate blocks. Click on the Block Flow option from the Tools drop-down menu, located at the top-right corner of the window. In the pop-up, you'll get the list of blocks added in your survey. Now click on any one of the Add Randomizer link to add a randomizer section. In the pop-up, drag and drop all the blocks within the newly added randomizer section. Select the maximum number (3 in this example) in the Randomly display drop-down menu and click on the Save button. Evenly present blocks logic toggle has been deprecated as of September 30th, 2024. You have successfully created a monadic test survey which will display the concepts to the respondent in a randomized order to get their response. Pros and cons to sequential monadic testing Link Copied Pros: Since each respondent is shown at least two concepts, the required sample size goes down, making the research more cost-effective. Since you require fewer respondents, this method is more suitable for researching niche markets. Fewer respondents also mean less time is needed to field the survey and collect data. Cons: Presenting multiple concepts at a time increases the survey's length, which affects the completion rate of the survey and introduces the risk of nonresponse bias to your research. The research may also be at risk of order bias unless the order of the concept is appropriately randomized. Extraction logic allows the survey to retain information from previously asked question/s and allows the survey authors to gain further insights for the selections made by respondents for a specific question. We have now paired this feature with Image selection question type where different logics can be applied to extract the images chosen by respondents in subsequent questions and rate their preferences. For example, consider a question in your survey, \"Please choose your favorite ice cream flavors from the given options.\" with the following options to choose from: Chocolate Vanilla Strawberry Butterscotch Based on respondents' answers, survey authors may ask further questions for the selected choice. You can set this up easily using Extraction Logic where the extracted options will appear with their corresponding images as provided in the previous question. In the provided example, the survey author has built logic to extract selected choices in the form of a star rating question. Assuming that the survey taker has chosen \"Chocolate\" and \"Butterscotch\" from the given options, here is how the next question will appear with their corresponding images. How do I setup extraction logic? To set up extraction: Go to: Login » Surveys (click on a survey) » Edit. Click on Logic for the question from which you wish to extract follow up questions. Select Extraction logic from the drop down. Select the options, you’d want to extract for the follow up questions. You can select from the following options: Selected Choices: All options selected by respondents will be used in the extracted question. Not Selected Choices: All options that are not selected by respondents will be used in the extracted question. Displayed Choices: All options that are displayed to the respondent will be extracted. Not Displayed Choices: All options that are hidden by show/hide logic and not displayed to the respondent will be extracted. All Choices: All choices will be extracted. Click on the drop-down next to 'to question type' and select the “Rating” >> “Star” option. Click on Save Extraction Logic button. The extracted question will appear below the source question. The respondents can rank the concepts or directly select the best concept. How to set up a comparison test in QuestionPro? Link Copied To set up a comparison test in your QuestionPro account, please follow the steps below: Go to: Surveys >> Edit. Add a closed ended question type to your survey. Link Copied Following are some question types which can be used for the comparison testing: Multiple choice(select one): You can use this question type to ask the likability of the respondent towards a specific text. Image Chooser (select one): You can use this question type to ask the likability of the respondent towards a specific image. Ordering(Rank Order): You can use this question type to ask the likability of the respondent towards a specific text/image by giving them the option to rank the answer choices. Basic Matrix(Multi-Point Scales): You can use this question type to ask the rating/satisfaction of the respondent towards various items by giving them the option to select the rating or answer option of each item mention in the question. Please refer to the You may also be interested in... section at the bottom of this document to know about all the question types provided by QuestionPro and can be used for the comparison testing. Pros and cons to comparison testing Link Copied Pros Comparison testing provides results that are easy to understand. This is a clear winner amongst the concepts since the respondents directly identify that they like a specific ad campaign better than the others or are willing to pay more for a particular product. Cons Although the results provided by a comparison test are easy to digest, they seldom offer real context about the true appeal of a concept There might be a specific detail about an ad campaign or a particular feature of a product that made it more appealing to the respondents. There are 3 ways you can create a survey using QuestionPro: Create a survey from scratch in your account Use a pre designed survey template Upload a survey using a microsoft word document The goal of creating a survey is to elicit holistic information from a target audience that provides enough data points to generate actionable insights. How do I create a survey? To create a survey from scratch, follow these simple steps: Go to: Login » My Surveys. Click on the new survey button. There are two ways of creating a survey: Create a survey from scratch: Click new survey » enter the survey name of your choice » click on the create survey button. Your survey will be created and you can begin adding question types that you prefer, and customize their settings in accordance to your survey goals. Use a template to create a survey: Click new survey » use a template » select the survey template of your choice. A pop-up will appear giving a preview of the selected template. Click on use this template button to create a survey using the selected survey template. If you like, you can customize the questions or answer options within the template. Upload a survey using a word document Click new survey » enter the survey name » click on the create survey button » click on Add questions from MS word. A pop-up will appear giving you the option to upload a word file or download a sample document. Click on Sample Microsoft Word Document button to download a sample file for reference. Once your file is ready for upload, drag and drop the file and click on upload to create your survey. <span>Inline frames not supported</span> Which is the most recommended survey creation method? When you are sure about what kind of survey you wish to create, you have a set goal and the questionnaire to gather relevant data, the ‘new survey’ method is the most recommended as it gives you the ability to design your survey exactly the way you want it to be. When you are not sure about what kind of survey you wish to create or the right questions to ask your target audience, you could always use the ‘templates’ and then edit/customize them accordingly.       "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Survey Preview Options",
      "summary": "The official help guide for using QuestionPro survey preview options.",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/survey-preview-options.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:38:41.917Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Survey Preview"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Survey Preview Survey preview allows you to test your survey easily. It allows you to test the user experience as to how the respondents would see and interact with your survey. With the survey preview, you can easily and repetitively test your survey with and without: logic, validation, and other settings. Survey preview makes testing your survey a breeze and the data collected can easily be deleted to start live data collection with a blank slate. How can I test my survey using the survey preview? You can use survey preview under Edit as well as the Distribute navigation menu under Surveys. Go to: Login » Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit You will find the blue preview button with the eye icon near the top right beside the live survey link. Clicking on the preview button will open the survey preview in a new tab. What are the survey preview options available? To make survey testing easy, following options are available: Select Block: You can directly jump to any of the blocks in the survey by selecting the required block. URL Variable: Use URL variable option for testing custom variables that can be passed via the survey URL, update the variable and value and test the survey. Validation: You can disable validation when testing a survey so you don't need to answer validated questions every time. Logic: You can disable logic to test the survey questions. Page Breaks: You can disable page breaks and look at all questions in a single page. Share Preview Link: Click on the share icon to share preview link for your survey with your fellow researchers and colleagues. You can enable password protection for shared URL. Preview Device: You can select to view how the survey look in a specific device. You can select from the desktop view or tab or mobile view. How can I identify responses saved during testing using the preview URL? Responses saved when using the preview survey option are saved with the text test_response in the external reference variable for every response. You can view this in the response viewer and this information is part of all raw data exports. Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Analytics Select the responses option from the drop down under dashboard. How can I delete these test responses saved using preview URL? Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Analytics Select the Delete option from under Manage Data dropdown. Under Delete, click on the Clear Responses option from the Clear All Test Responses section. Test data will be permanently deleted and cannot be retrieved.       "content": "Survey Edit and Collaboration 2025 - FAQ We've supercharged our survey-building platform with a new tech stack and significant infrastructure enhancements! This isn't just an update; it's a complete overhaul designed to elevate your survey creation workflow and bring you a more powerful, reliable, and seamless experience. Blazing-Fast Performance: Create, build, and publish complex surveys quicker than ever before. Enhanced Stability: Enjoy a more robust and dependable platform with consistent and uninterrupted performance. Future-Ready Foundation: These upgrades lay the groundwork for exciting new features and capabilities coming soon! We'll start moving users to the new infrastructure in phases, with all users transitioned to the updated platform by December 2025. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video What is new? Draft and Publish Survey Modes: All new surveys are now created in draft mode, providing a dedicated space for you to build and thoroughly test your survey before it goes live. When you're ready to begin collecting data, simply publish your survey to launch it. This new process ensures your surveys are polished and ready for your audience. Collaborate and Build Surveys Together: Our platform allows you and your team to collaborate on surveys in real-time. You can easily work with users across your team or entire organization. The new real-time presence indicator lets you see who is actively working on a survey, so you can avoid conflicting changes and stay aligned with your collaborators. Survey Versions: Our platform now features robust survey versioning to give you greater control over your surveys. Every action is logged, providing a clear history of changes. You can now revert to any previous version of your survey, ensuring you have a complete record and the flexibility to undo changes whenever needed. What happens to my existing surveys? No change! We're transitioning to an upgraded platform, but there's no action required for your current surveys. All of your existing projects are safe and will remain unchanged. Going forward, any new surveys you create will benefit from the enhanced features and performance of our new architecture. Do we need any training or learning to use the new upgraded platform? We've made a significant effort to ensure this upgrade is seamless for you. The user experience will remain familiar, with only a few intuitive upgrades designed to make building surveys even easier. This means there's no need for any formal training, and you'll find there is no learning curve to navigate the new platform. You can continue creating surveys just as you always have. To ensure a smooth transition, rollout plan includes: Webinars: We will host webinars and live Q&A sessions to address any specific questions you may have. Self-Guided Resources: A refreshed knowledge base with articles and short videos that demonstrate the new features. How can I tell if my survey is on the new or old infrastructure? For surveys created on our new and improved infrastructure, you'll now see the Draft and Publish modes clearly displayed in the top right corner. Live survey link for new surveys will only be available in published mode. URL will not be available in draft mode. Data collection is paused when a survey is in draft mode. For all surveys on our legacy infrastructure, the live survey link will remain always visible. The draft and publish options will not be available for these older surveys. Was the article helpful?       "id": "surveys-v2-v3-test",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Getting Started Test",
      "summary": "Standard question types",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/surveys-v2-v3-test.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:38:56.916Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Standard question types"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Standard question types QuestionPro has 29 standard (basic) questions to build a perfect survey. Here is the list of standard question types with a brief description. For detailed description, click on each question type to visit the complete help page. Multiple choice : Multiple choice questions are used where respondents can select an answer from multiple options displayed below the question. Multiple choice (select one) : This question allows your respondents to select a single answer option. Radio buttons are used for single select type questions. If the question has a long list of answer options, for example, a list of countries, then drop-down menu can be used for a multiple choice select one question. Multiple choice (select many) : This question allows your respondents to select multiple answer options. Checkboxes are used for multiple select type questions. If the question has a long list of answer options (more than 10 options), then select list can be set as the answer type from the settings. Text: This is an open-ended text question where users can provide inputs in single word, short sentences, or paragraphs. Comment box : This is an open-ended text question where users can input longer text responses. For example, you can use a comment box question for collecting feedback where respondents can enter inputs in paragraphs. Email address : This question is used for email addresses. When validated, only valid email addresses will be accepted. Contact information : This question is used for gathering basic contact information, including first name, last name, email address, mailing address, and phone number. Graphical rating : Rating questions allow respondents to compare different items using a common rating scale or to compare a list of items to each other. Star rating : This question allows respondents to rank attributes on a visual scale of stars. Text slider : A text slider is an interactive question where rating values (dissatisfied-satisfied, unlikely-likely, poor-excellent, and many more) are represented on a bar. The respondents can drag the slider on the bar to set their rating value. Push to social : This question allows respondents to rate an item with the help of star rating, then, depending on the rating, a recommendation is displayed to the respondents to share their experience on social networking sites including Facebook, Twitter, and Yelp. Numeric slider : Similar to text slider, users can slide the bar to rate items. The rating value will be a numeric range. Thumbs Up/Down : This question allows you to ask the respondents whether they like or dislike an item asked in the question. The respondents can use a thumbs up or thumbs down image to help define the rating options. Smiley - rating : The smiley question is a 5-point rating scale, that allows you to capture sentiments of your respondents towards your products or services in the form of smiley images intended to represent a range of sentiments from negative to positive and vice versa. Basic Matrix: A matrix question is a group of individual questions that share the same answer options. Multi-point scales : This question can be used when we want the respondents to select only one option for each row by clicking on the radio buttons. Checkbox/Multi-select : This question allows the respondents to select multiple answer options from each row in the matrix question. Spreadsheet : This question uses text boxes in the answer option that allow the respondents to enter text inputs. Ordering: Questions scale Rank Order allows you to qualify a set of brands or products based on a specific attribute or characteristic. Rank order : Rank order scaling questions allow a certain set of brands or products to be ranked based upon a specific attribute or characteristic. Constant sum : A constant sum question permits respondents to enter numeric values for list of items that are then summed for a total value. This is used to see the allocation of a value into several items. For example, the revenue generated from different regions (North America, Europe, APAC) summing up to the total revenue. Drag and drop : Respondents can drag and drop the answer choices to re-order them as per their preferential order. Image chooser : Image questions allow you to show a visualization of the answer options for respondents to review and select. Instead of selecting a radio button, respondents can scroll over the image and pick the one that best suits their answer. Image chooser (select one) : This is a multiple choice single select question where you can add images in the answer options along with the text. Radio buttons are used for single select type questions. Answer option orientation can either be vertical (default setting) or horizontal. Image chooser (select many) : This is a multiple choice multi-select question which has images next to the answer options. Check boxes are used for Image Chooser multiple select type questions. Answer option orientation can either be vertical (default setting) or horizontal. Rating : Ask users to select one or more images from a set of images presented. Static content Presentation text: Presentation text is text that can be used as messages, instructions or headings in your survey. It can also be used to visually separate sections in your survey. The section headings can also be used in the reports to help visually see groups of questions. Section heading: To group the survey into sections and to enhance the look of the survey you can add section/page headers to the survey. Section sub-heading: To group the survey into sections and to enhance the look of the survey you can add section/page headers to the survey. Presentation/ heading question type is used only for display purpose. No user input is expected. Misc Date / Time : This question provides a drop-down menu for date selection. You can also enable display time to where respondent can enter the time. This question allows the respondents to enter a date as an input, for example, birthdate or schedule meeting (date and time). reCAPTCHA : This question allows to validate the respondent is an actual respondent and not a bot designed to submit random answers to a form or survey. Calendar : This question is similar to a date/time question. But, instead of a drop-down, the respondents can pick a date from a calendar. Maps : It is much easier for the respondents to select their location by using the clickable interactive maps. Just by rolling the mouse over a certain part of the map, the respondent can instantly select that area from the pop-up text, links, and images. Timer : Page timer question can be used to manage and track the time spent by respondents on each page. Page timer can be used on tests, quizzes or surveys where you want respondents to spend specific amount of time. Page timer should be added as the last question on page. An automatic page break is applied on the page timer question. Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.",

        "In large-scale surveys, closed-ended questions take less time from the interviewer, the participant and the researcher, and so are a less expensive survey method. The response rate is higher with surveys that use closed-ended questions than with those that use open-ended questions.",

      ],
      "content": "Multiple choice question types (closed-ended) Multiple choice closed-ended questions: Multiple choice closed-ended questions allow respondents to choose one or more answers from a given list of answer options. You can define the answer options of your own choice or you can use a pre-existing set of dichotomous answers, such as yes/no, true/false, or ranking scale response options. It also allows you to add an \"other\" option where the respondents can fill in their own answer choice. You can also add the NA (not applicable) option. What are the advantages of multiple choice closed-ended questions? Multiple choice closed-ended questions are more easily analyzed. Every answer can be given a number or value so that a statistical interpretation can be assessed. Closed-ended questions are also better suited for computer analysis. If open-ended questions are analyzed quantitatively, the qualitative information is reduced to coding and answers tend to lose some of their initial meaning. Because of the simplicity of closed-ended questions, this kind of loss is avoided. Multiple choice closed-ended questions can be more specific, thus more likely to communicate similar meanings. Because open-ended questions allow respondents to use their own words, it is difficult to compare the meanings of the responses. In large-scale surveys, closed-ended questions take less time from the interviewer, the participant and the researcher, and so are a less expensive survey method. The response rate is higher with surveys that use closed-ended questions than with those that use open-ended questions. Answer types: Radio buttons: A radio button or option button is a type of graphical user interface element that allows the respondents to choose only one option from a predefined set of options. Check boxes: A check box (checkbox, tickbox, or tick box) is a graphical user interface element (widget) that permits the respondents to make multiple selections from a number of options. Drop-down: A drop-down list is a user interface control GUI element, similar to a list box, which allows the respondents to choose one value from a list. When a drop-down list is inactive, it displays a single value. When activated, it displays (drops down) a list of values, from which the respondents may select one. Select list: A select list box is a GUI widget that allows the user to select one or more items from a list contained within a static, multiple line text box. The respondent can click inside the box on an item to select it, sometimes in combination with the shift key or Control key in order to make multiple selections. Validation: Once you add questions to a survey, by default, the validation option is turned off. If the validation option is disabled, respondents can continue with the survey without answering the question. By enabling the validation option, you can see a new pop-up will appear with these options - force response and request response. When you select the Request response option, the respondent gets a pop-up in case a question is not answered, requesting to answer the question or continue the survey without answering. Force response option makes a question mandatory. Select at most/exactly/at least: You can put validation on the number of answer options that a respondent must select before proceeding to the next question. For example, if you want the respondent to select a minimum of 3 answer options, then choose select at least from the drop-down and set the value as 3. Custom validation messages: You can set a customized validation message as well. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.",
