    },
    {
      "id": "conjoint-analysis-best-practice-mplementation",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Conjoint Best Practices",
      "summary": "Conjoint Analysis - Best Practice Implementation FAQ",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/conjoint-analysis-best-practice-mplementation.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:48:14.908Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Conjoint Analysis - Best Practice Implementation FAQ"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [
        "Create a screening/profiling survey and use simple \"Multiple Choice (Multiple Select)\" to determine viability of attributes - \"Pick 6 of 20\" etc.",
        "Use TURF Analysis to pick the Top 5 or 6 attributes with the highest reach.",
        "Then as a secondary wave, run the conjoint study on highest reach attributes."
      ],
      "content": "Conjoint Analysis - Best Practice Implementation FAQ Please be aware that opinions and suggestions given here are exactly that -- Our opinions and suggestions. Conjoint Analysis is a fairly complicated model and while there is no single solution this FAQ should be considered as a broad opinion trying to encompass as many scenarios as possible. Many of our suggestions and methods here are OUR opinions and may contradict other research executed for specific scenarios. How do I determine how many concepts per screen should I ask? In general for internet based (self-administered) surveys, asking users to choose between more than 3 or 4 concepts is generally not reasonable. The cognitive stress placed on users far outweighs the extensibility in the utility calculation. The number of concepts that you choose also depends upon the number attributes that is being measured. For example, if you are measuring more than 3 attributes, the users have to understand and comprehend product (that is comprised or 3 or more attributes) and then make a decision between the different concepts. In general, however if you have 3 or less attributes, you can go with 3 concepts per task. However, if you have more than 3 attributes, we would suggest that you do not put in more than 2 concepts per task. How are the tasks (profiles) created and displayed? Is it pre-defined or does the system create concepts and present them to the user? The concepts are randomly created and displayed to the user. You can use the \"Prohibited Pairs\" feature to make sure certain pairs of levels are never part of the same concept. But, the model generally is to keep the creation of the concepts random - so as to explore as varied a possibility to gain insight into individual utilities. The alogrithm for computing a profile is as follows: System will choose random levels for each attribute to create the first concept. Subsequent concepts (for each task) will be created such that the levels are not repeated. In cases where this is not possible, the levels will be repeated. Under no circumstances will the system display two identical profiles The system will also take into account User-Defined \"Prohibited Pairs\" to make sure profiles that are created. Can there be an over-reliance on a particular attribute like \"Price\" or \"Cost?\" Yes. In almost all conjoint studies, Price and Cost will be the primary determining factor. If previous conjoint studies have shown an over-reliance on Price/Cost factors, it may also make sense to conduct a study WITHOUT price as one of the components as a test-run. While this approach will not work for price-sensitivity testing, it will however elimitate the \"Price-Fixation\" attitude and give you deeper insight into the other attributes. Another mechanism for solving the \"Price-Fixation\" (where users show a tedency to heavily rely on price i.e. the cheapest price always wins) is to do price-bands -- i.e. where the levels in the Cost/Price attribute is defined as a range -- Eg. ($1.99 - $2.99) This approach may be able to help in \"de-focusing\" cost and allowing users to pay equal attention to other factors. How do I determine how many tasks I should have the user complete? Our experience has shown that there is a precipitious drop-out rate after about 15 tasks. Unless there is a strong personal incentive for the end-users to complete the survey, we would suggest to keep the number of tasks to under 15 especially in cases where users are volunteering to take surveys. Please keep in mind that conjoint product selection is a little more involved than simply \"answering a survey question\" -- users have to comprehend each of the attributes/concepts and then make a choice. This is a lot more involved than say choosing \"Male/Female\" on a gender question. On the lower side, we would suggest that 6-8 tasks be the minium for a conjoint model with 3 attributes. The more attributes you have, the more number of tasks users have to fill out. It is obvious that it's a balancing act between the number of tasks, concepts per task and the total number of attributes/levels than need to be displayed. Two factors determine the overall utility: Concepts Per Task Total #of Tasks The system provides the \"Concept Simulator\" - with the concept simulator you can see the TOTAL number of times a particular level will be displayed (given the total number of respondents) - See Concept Simulator in the References section below. What about the \"None\" option? How does having the \"none\" option affect data calculation and reliability? If the \"None\" option is enabled, the utility calculation takes into account that NONE of the options was selected. The utility calculation fundamentally relies on the number of times a particular level is displayed to the user compared to the number of times a level is part of a chosen concept. If the \"none\" option is selected, then the utilities for all the levels in the options (that were not selected) will go down. What are the other implications of enabling the \"none\" options? From a practical standpoint, in some cases, where we've seen an over-emphasis on price/cost or a single level, the None option is selected whenever the emphasized level is not present in one of the random concepts. In such cases, it may not make sense to enable the \"none\" option and force users to choose the \"best\" option that they are presented with. How many levels can I have within each attribute? How many attributes can I have? Any guidelines? From a technical standpoint, the system does NOT impose any limitations. You can have unlimited attributes and unlimited levels within each attribute. However, from a practical standpoint, it is unreasonable to have more than 4-6 attributes, and about 3-4 levels per attribute. Our suggestion would be to keep the number of attributes to under 5 and try and seek about 3 levels for each attribute. I have a lot of Attributes that I'd like to test out (more than 5-6) - what can I do? Like mentioned above, the system does not limit the number of attributes. However from a practical presentation standpoint, it really does not make sense to have choices with more than 5-6 attributes because of the cognitive stress involved. However, if you do have a case where you'd like to test out 10-20 attributes we would suggest you do this as a two part project: Create a screening/profiling survey and use simple \"Multiple Choice (Multiple Select)\" to determine viability of attributes - \"Pick 6 of 20\" etc. Use TURF Analysis to pick the Top 5 or 6 attributes with the highest reach. Then as a secondary wave, run the conjoint study on highest reach attributes. Total concept combinations should not exceed 10,000. For example, if there are 4 features, with 10, levels each, the total combinations reach the 10,000 which is the maximum we can support. License This feature is available with the following license : Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.",

      "id": "conjoint-prohibited-pairs",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Conjoint Prohibited Pairs",
      "summary": "Prohibited Concepts - How to?",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/conjoint-prohibited-pairs.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:48:29.905Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Prohibited Concepts in Conjoint - How to?"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Prohibited Concepts in Conjoint - How to? Prohibited Concepts allow you to make the Concepts (Attributes and Levels) more realistic while doing conjoint study. It allows for defining \"Rules\" that the Conjoint Module will follow when deciding on what Attributes and Levels to make up a Concept. You can create as many \"Pairs\" as you want and the Engine will never display those levels that have been marked as \"Prohibited\" in the same concept (as a product) for the user to choose. You can also directly prohibit a concept by providing the levels of that concept. How to set up prohibited concepts? Go to: Login » Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit » For your conjoint question, click on the Prohibited Concepts setting link. Add your prohibited concept and save. How do I \"Remove\" a Prohibited Concepts? To remove an \"Existing Prohibited Concepts, simply change the Drop Down Combo-Box to \"Select\", and then Save your changes. This will automatically remove that Prohibited Concepts. Important: It is very important that you simulate (Using the Concept Simulator) after defining your Prohibited Concepts. It is entirely possible that the combination of the Prohibited Concepts that you have defined leave fewer alternatives than the number of \"Concepts Per Task\" -- In such a case the engine will error out and the user will not be able to complete the survey. Please simulate at least 1000 respondents (Using the Concept Simulator) to make sure that your design is orthogonal enough. In order to avoid this, please only use the bare minimum prohibited pairs, to achieve maximum orthogonal design. Remember, the idea behind Conjoint is to \"discover\" trade offs. Kindly Note: QuestionPro replaces the prohibited concept with a non-prohibited ones and keep replacing until we get a unique non-prohibited concept. Make sure prohibited count is less than task and concept, if it is more than task and concept we will throw a validation error as below: Simulated Concept choices should never be '0'. Browser Title: The Browser Title lets you define the text displayed in the title bar of your survey webpage. Social Network Toolbar: Switch on this option to include social buttons for sharing the survey via email, Facebook, or Twitter. Auto-advance option is only available with the classic survey mode and will be disabled for focus display mode. Auto-submit: Auto submit survey answers to make it easy for respondents to answer a survey.         }

      "steps": [],
      "content": "Progress Bar - How To? Progress bar helps your respondents see their progress when answering the survey. A progress bar is particularly helpful for long surveys where respondents are likely to get anxious on how much progress they have made in completing the survey. Depending on your surveys, QuestionPro offers a few different styles of progress bar that offer the best experience for respondents. Where can I change the progress bar style or remove the progress bar completely? By default, progress bar is set to style Modern for your surveys. To change or remove the progress bar: Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit Go to the Design tab. For any selected layout, you will find the Progress Bar under Settings. Here you can select from different styles or select None to remove the progress bar completely. Click on Save button to save your changes. Progress bar style: Modern Default progress bar for surveys is set to Modern. Progress bar will be displayed at the top of the survey in the selected theme color. On the progress bar, slats are displayed at 20% breaking the bar in 5 equal portions. Percentage of survey questions loaded are denoted on the progress bar in the theme color. Progress bar style: Linear Progress bar will be displayed under the survey title with percentage of survey questions loaded denoted on the progress bar in the theme color. Progress bar style: Classic Progress bar will be displayed on the survey body with percentage of survey questions loaded denoted on the progress bar in the theme color along with the percentage readout. With the classic progress bar, you can also select the progress bar position at top or bottom of the survey and also specify left, center, or right alignment. Additionally you can select the position to be sticky or non-sticky. With the sticky progress bar, the bar will stick to the top or bottom of the page once respondents scroll on the page. This helps keeping the progress bar always in sight of the respondents. When the progress bar is set to non-sticky, the progress bar will move along with the scroll on page Classic progress bar is compatible with our accessible theme and is 508 compliant for accessibility What are the progress bar scale options? Progress bar scale options are currently available only with our classic progress bar. You can choose from the following options: Percent Questions Loaded: Will display the percentage of questions loaded. For example: 10% Questions Answered: Will display the percentage of questions answered. For example: 10% Count Questions Answered: Will display the count of questions answered. For example: 2 of 10 Page Number: Will display the page number. For example: Page 2 of 5 Progress bar on my survey shows 100% even without answering a question? Progress bar displays the percent of survey questions loaded. For a single page survey, the modern progress bar will display 100% loaded. For a single page survey, you can use the classic progress bar style and change the progress bar scale to questions answered instead of questions loaded. Can I skip the progress bar from the first page of the survey? Yes. You can switch on the toggle for Skip Progress Bar on First Page. Navigation buttons are form submission buttons displayed on the survey. Survey/Response Submit Buttons Start Button: The start button text for the survey is displayed on the first page of a multi-page survey. For a single page survey, the finish button text is displayed. Next Button: The next button text is displayed on the second page of the survey and on subsequent pages until the last page of the survey. If a survey has just two pages, only the start button text and the finish button text will be displayed on the first and the last page of the survey. Finish Button: The finish button text is displayed on the last page of the survey. Clicking on this button submits the complete response for a survey. Exit Button: The Exit button is displayed on the header of the survey and allows users to exit the survey. You can set up the exit URL so clicking on the exit button will take the respondents out of a survey and to a website/page of your choice. How to change the default text used for the submit buttons? To set default button text for all new surveys for your organization, Go to: Login » Organization » Settings Click on the Settings button. On the pop-up, update and save the button text. Once saved, all new surveys will be created with the set button text for your organization.       ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Accessible Layout - 508 compliance What is accessibility? Accessibility can be viewed as the \"ability to access\" and benefit from a system or entity. The concept focuses on enabling access for people with disabilities, or enabling access through the use of assistive technology; however, research and development in accessibility brings benefits to everyone. How can I use the accessible layout for my survey? Link Copied Go to: Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit » Design Under Survey Layout, select the Accessible layout. Which are the question types compatible and supported for accessibility? Link Copied For accessibility, we want to make the surveys as easy as possible to answer. Keeping the surveys simple should be the primary goal. To achive this, certain question types like drag and drop, image hotspot or heatmap, and complex questions like flex matrix, etc. aren't supported. Settings like auto-advance and auto-submit along with tools like exit survey button are disabled on 508 compliant or accessible surveys to ease navigation through the survey. Following is a complete list of question types with accessibilitiy support: Basic Question Types: Multiple Choice Select One Select Many Drop-down Menu Text Comment Box Single Row Text Email Address Contact Information Graphical Rating Star Rating Smiley - Rating Thumbs Up/Down Image Chooser Select One Select Many Rating Ordering Rank Order Constant Sum Basic Matrix Multi-Point Scales Multi-Select Spreadsheet Static Content Presentation Text Section Heading Section Sub-Heading Misc Date / Time Calendar Advanced Question Types Customer Satisfaction Net Promoter Score Pricing Analysis Van Westendorp Data Reference Reference Data Lookup Table Logic Custom Logic Engine How do I know if a question type is accessibility compliant? Once accessible theme is enabled for a survey, we display accessibility icon on all question types that are accessibility compliant. This makes it very easy to identify question types that support accessibility when adding questions. Go to: Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit » Add Question. What about images on the survey? Link Copied We recommend minimizing the use of images on surveys however, when required, images can be used along with alt-text for images. alt-text is required for images so screen-readers can read and explain what the image is about. Guidelines for alt-text: Don't leave alt-text blank Alt-text should provide context and describe the image Avoid long alt-text. To describe the image in details, use presentation text and keep the alt-text short. If you are using a logo for your survey, click on the accessibility icon to add alt-text for the image. For all image supported questions, you will see the accessibility icon next to the image, click on the icon to update alt-text for these images. If an image is added using our rich text editor, you can right click on the image and add alt-text. What is the limit on the number of characters in the title of the survey? Link Copied As per accessibility guidelines, title of a survey should be between 30 to 60 characters. We strongly advise to keep the title under 60 characters. For accessibility compliance, we restrict the number of characters including spaces to 128. If title exceeds 128 charactes and you enable the accessible theme, title will be truncated to 128 characters. Which screen readers are supported for accessibility? Link Copied We have tested and confirm support for the following screen readers: Screen Reader Version Platform NVDA (Nonvisual Desktop Access) 2019.1 or later Windows JAWS (Job Access with Speech) 16 or later Windows Microsoft Narrator 2020 or later Windows VoiceOver macOS Catalina or later Mac OS X, iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch, iPods, and Apple TV ChromeVox Chrome OS 56 or above ChromeOS or, with a speech processor, Linux, Mac, Windows License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful? When you create a new survey, the back button and the exit link are enabled by default. To disable them, Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit » Design. Click on Settings.       "content": "Focus Layout Focus layout allows you to display a single question per page. With focus layout, you can eliminate all distractions and allow respondents to focus on a single question at a time. This mode improves survey response time as well as the quality of data collected. Here is a glimpse at how focus mode works: How to enable focus mode? Go to: Login » My Surveys (select a survey) » Edit Click on the Design tab. Click on the Focus mode option under Display Mode What is the classic display mode? By default, your survey display mode is set to classic. In the classic survey mode, all questions will be displayed as you have set them up under the workspace area. Classic survey display mode considers all page-breaks you have set on the survey. Multiple questions will be displayed on a page until a page-break is identified. What is the focus display mode? Focus display mode will display a single question per page. With focus mode, there are no distractions and respondents can concentrate on just one question at a time. Focus mode inproves data quality as well as speed at which respondents can answer the survey. Why can I not enable focus mode for my survey? Certain question types cannot be displayed when focus mode is enabled. Question types with steps or specific interactions don't work with the focus mode. If these question types are added on a survey, we don't allow to switch on focus mode for the survey. Following question types are not supported: HotSpot HeatMap Store Locator Homunculus Tubepulse Live cast Page Timer Signature Verified Signature If any of these question types are used, focus mode will be disabled and you will not be able to switch on focus mode. When focus mode is enabled, responses are submitted automatically for certain questions. Auto-submit response setting is enabled by default. We recommend keeping this setting enabled to improve respondent experience.       "content": "Survey Layout Survey layout plays an essential role in your research. With QuestionPro, you can create beautiful, engaging, and interactive surveys to collect reliable, accurate, and quality data for your study. What are the different survey layout options? For your surveys, you can choose from the following survey layouts: Classic: Classic survey layout displays multiple questions on page. You can insert page breaks where you would like to break questions in classic layout. Use the classic layout when it is important to display a group of interdependent questions on the same page. Focus: Focus survey layout keeps focus on a single question reducing distractions and improves the speed of response when collecting feedback. Visual: Visual layout allows you to create beautiful surveys with image background for every question. Visual layout will display a single question on each page and reduces any distractions allowing for improved data quality and reducing survey fatigue. Accessible: Accessible layout allows you to create surveys that ensure inclusivity, allowing everyone including people with disabilities to participate. Using accessible layout isn't just about ethics; it provides a more acurate and representative understanding of your entier customer base. Go to: My Surveys (select a survey) » Edit » Design Select the layout you prefer and save. How to change the default survey layout for my account? Focus survey layout is set as the default survey layout for your surveys. Any new surveys you create will be created with the default survey layout. To change the default survey layout, Go to: Login » My Account (Top Right Corner) Under My Account, scroll down to survey settings and here you can set the default survey layout for your account. How to change the default display mode for the entire organization? Go to: Login » Organization » Settings Under Organization settings, click on the Settings button. On the popup, scroll to the Display Mode option and select your preferred display mode and Save.       "content": "Survey Layout - Visual What is the visual layout? Collecting feedback should not be boring! Visual mode assimilates the philosophy of design driven feedback and supports creating surveys that are beautiful and engaging on every interaction. Visual mode enables you to customize background images for each question individually. You also have full control over where and how the question is displayed on screen without worrying about the devices your customers will provide feedback from. Visual mode seamlessly handles the display across devices like laptops, tablets, mobiles, and even TV's and other displays and at the same time makes it extremely easy for respondents to provide feedback. Click to download video How to enable visual layout? Go to: Login » My Surveys (select a survey) » Edit » Design Under the design tab, under Survey Layout, select the Visual layout. Click on the Customize tab to further customize the theme. Following customization options are available Background: You can select any image you like and this image will be set as the default background for all questions in the survey. Layout: You can select from the following question layout options Full: Full image background will be used with questions in the foreground. Left: Survey content will be displayed on the left with the image displayed on the right half. Right: Survey content will be displayed on the right with the image on the right half. Half: Image will be displayed in the top half and survey content in the bottom half. Alignment: Horizontal alignment options for the survey content to be displayed left, center, or right aligned. Widget color - Selected: This will be the primary theme color for the layout and will be used for buttons, progress bar, etc. Widget color - Unselected: This will be the secondary theme color and used for unselected response options as well as secondary buttons like the back button. Overlay Style: You can select and customize from the following overlay options for better legibility. Choose light color overlay for darker fonts and dark color overlay for lighter fonts. Fill: Consistent overlay will be applied. You can select the color as well as opacity for the overlay. Gradient: Gradient style overlay with density reducing vertically from top to bottom will be applied. None: No overlay will be applied. Font: You can select the font for your survey as well as the font colors. Foreground font color: Selected foreground color will be used for the question and answer option font color. Highlight font color: Selected highlight font color will be used for the text on primary buttons. How to select custom background image for questions? For the question you would like to customize, click on the Settings link to open the settings panel. Select the Visual settings option. Visual settings will only be available and visible if visual layout is selected. Now you can select a custom background. To apply this new background to all the following questions, select the sticky background toggle. Why can I not enable visual display mode for my survey? Certain question types cannot be displayed when visual mode is enabled. Question types with steps or specific interactions don't work with the visual mode. If these question types are added on a survey, we don't allow to switch on visual mode for the survey. Following question types are currently not supported: Basic Question Types Text Slider Bipolar Matrix Spreadsheet Matrix Advanced Question Types HotSpot HeatMap Store Locator Homunculus Tubepulse Live cast Page Timer Signature Verified Signature Conjoint Max-Diff Side-By-Side Matrix Complex Grid/Flex Matrix If any of these question types are used, visual mode will be disabled and you will not be able to switch on the visual display mode. Classic display mode will be used for surveys with above question types.       "content": "Workspace URL What is a workspace URL? Workspace URL is the base URL for all your survey links. Workspace URL is useful to improve brand identity and recognition for your survey links. How do I update my workspace URL? Workspace URLs are set for every organization. You can update the workspace URL under your organization settings Please note that you can only have one workspace URL for your organization. If you update the workspace URL, it will be updated for your entire organization and applied to all surveys. DO_NOT update workspace URL if you have live surveys with ongoing data collection. Go to: Login » Organization » Settings Click on the Settings button On the popup you can update the workspace URL if required For your organization the workspace URL will be set as the base URL for all surveys Can I remove the workspace URL? No. Workspace URL adds security and individual brand identity for your survey URLs. You cannot remove the workspace URLs. What happens if I change the workspace URL? We recommend not changing the workspace URL if you have surveys actively collecting data. However, if you still wish to change the workspace URL. All links with the earlier workspace URL will stop working. You will need to redistribute the survey with the new workspace URL.       "id": "branching-skip-logic-new",

      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/branching-skip-logic-new.html",

      "headings": [

          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Branching or skip logic"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Branching or skip logic Branching or skip logic gives you the ability to create “intelligent” surveys, meaning respondents can answer relevant questions based on their answers to screening questions. Skip Logic interrupts the default survey flow and redirects the respondent to another location which can be another question in your survey, another survey, thank you page, or terminate survey. How do I add branching or skip logic on a question? Before adding any logic, you should set up the entire survey with all of the questions. This will make it easier to check that the logic is correct when testing the survey. Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit. Select the question you wish to apply logic and click on logic. On the popup, skip logic will be selected by default. For each answer option, you can set the If selected, Jump to question logic. If you wish, you can update Piping Text and also assign it to a Custom Variable. Piping text can be used on any question after this question. Assigning the piping text to a variable will allow you to use it throughout the rest of the survey and also with reporting. Click here to know more details about piping text. Setting up Default Branching is optional. Default Branching logic will be executed in case none of the branching options are selected. Click Save Logic button to complete the set up. When logic is added for a question, a page break is automatically inserted. This page break is necessary for branching to function properly and cannot be removed. What are the different jump to options? For each answer option, respondents can be directed or skipped to the following Jump To options: Survey Questions: You can select from any of the questions that are after the source question. Terminate Survey: Select this option if you wish to terminate the survey response. Respondents will be directed to the terminate survey page and the response will be marked as a Terminated response. Thank You page: Select this option if you wish to direct respondents to the Thank You page. Response will be marked as a completed response. Chain Survey: Select this option to take respondents to a different survey. <span>Inline frames not supported</span> License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?     {
      "id": "compound-branching-new",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Compound Branching",
      "summary": "Compound Branching Logic",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/compound-branching-new.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:50:41.903Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Compound Branching"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Compound Branching Compound logic can be used to set up logic based on answers to multiple questions as well as system variables. It is also used for delayed branching where you can apply logic based on answers to a question or multiple questions that were answered earlier in the survey. Consider this example: Q1: How often do you visit our restaurant? Q2: Which city do you live in? Compound branching logic should be applied to question Q2 based on the options selected in Q1 and Q2. Q3: To be answered only by respondents who are first-time visitors and live in Seattle. Q4: To be answered only by respondents who are first-time visitors and live in New York. Q5: To be answered only by respondents who visit often and live in Seattle. Q6 To be answered only by respondents who visit often and live in New York. Q7: General question to be answered by everybody How do I add compound logic? Before adding any logic, you should set up the entire survey with all of the questions. This will make it easier to check that the logic is correct when testing the survey. Go to: Login » Surveys (click on a survey) » Edit Select the question you wish to apply logic and click on Logic. Select the Compound Branching option. Start creating a criteria by adding a name. Now set up condition checks for the criteria. Select the question, select the comparison operator, and select the answer option. You can add another condition by clicking on the plus icon. Once you have added all conditions, select the branching destination for If criteria is met, jump to. You can create additional criteria by repeating the above steps. You have to select a question where the default logic should point. So when no criteria are matched, the selected question will be the destination for the logic. Click on Save Logic.       "id": "dynamic-quota-control",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Response Based Quota Control",
      "summary": "Control survey distribution and how many respondents you want for each option specified.",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/dynamic-quota-control.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:50:52.886Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Response Based Quota Control"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Response Based Quota Control With quota control, you can limit the responses received for answer options in your survey. For example, we are conducting a research study across different cities and want to limit the number of responses per city, we can do so by setting quota for responses per city. How to enable quota control? Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit. For the question you wish to set quota on, click on Logic Select Quota Control from the logic dropdown menu Enter quota for options, select the quota overlimit action, and save. What are the logic options available once quota is met? For Quota Control, you can set the jump to condition to either go to the Quota Overlimit page or you can hide the option once the quota is full. Quota Overlimit: If respondents select an option where quota is met, they will be taken to the quota overlimit page and the response will be terminated. Quota Full - Hide Option: Once quota is met, respondents will not see the option as part of selection and can still select other options and continue with the survey. Where can I edit the message displayed on the quota overlimit page? Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit » Finish Options Under Finish Options you can edit the message for the quota overlimit page. What is 'Minimum Quota'? Link Copied 'Minimum Quota' option is only available and applicable to Multi-select question type. When 'Minimum Quota' is enabled for a multi-select question, if the respondent selects more than one option but one of the options' quota is full and the other options they select would allow them to continue, the survey will not terminate and the user will be allowed to continue to the next question. For example, for the question given below, if the respondent only selects Visa when the quota is full the survey will terminate for over quota. If the respondent selects Visa and Mastercard and only the Visa quota is met, both the answers will be recorded in the data and the respondent will continue to next question. Watch this video for a live walkthrough of the setup. <span>Inline frames not supported</span> Click to download video License This feature is available with the following licenses : Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "summary": "Dynamic text or comments box",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/dynamic-comment-based-on-logic.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:50:57.906Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Dynamic text or comments box"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Dynamic text or comments box Dynamic Text/Comments boxes are used to collect open-ended comments from the respondents when they select options. This allows you to collect additional information about the option selected by the respondent. Typically, this can be used in the following scenario: Which is your favorite smartphone brand? Apple Samsung Motorola OnePlus A text box should appear to specify the reason for choosing the option when the respondent selects any of the options mentioned above. How do I enable Dynamic Comments on my survey? There are two ways of adding the dynamic text/comments on your survey: 1. Directly from the question: Go to: Login » Surveys (Click on a survey) » Edit. Click on Add Questions and add all questions in your survey. Hover over an option and click on the corresponding arrow. Select Dynamic Comment Box and enter the label - \"why?\" Similarly, you can add dynamic text boxes to other options too. 2. From the question logic: Go to: Login » Surveys (Click on a survey) » Edit. Click on Add Questions and add all questions in your survey. Hover over the question on which you wish to apply logic and click on logic. A pop-up will appear. Click on Dynamic Text/Comments. By default, Dynamic Text Box is disabled for each option. Go to the answer option you wish to enable dynamic comment, and select Enabled from the drop-down. A comment box will appear on the right side, type the label for the dynamic comment box here - \"Why?\" Similarly, you can enable dynamic text boxes for the remaining options too. Click on Save Logic How does dynamic text/comment box work while taking survey? While taking the survey, if the respondent chooses an option, the dynamic text box appears below the last answer option. For example, if the respondent selects Apple then, a text box with label \"Why?\" appears below the last answer option. In the comment box, the respondent can type the reason for selecting Apple as the favorite smartphone brand. How are comments entered in the dynamic text box captured in the report? Comments entered by the respondents in the dynamic text box will be automatically included in your Report as a separate field that follows the question it was applied to. To download report: Go to: Login » Surveys (Select a survey) » Analytics » Dashboard. Click on Download drop-down. Under Download Raw Data section, you can download the report in Excel or CSV format. In the downloaded report, you can see the column named Dynamic Comment with all the comments entered by the respondents. Question types that support Dynamic Text/Comments box. Dynamic text/comments box can be enabled for the following question types: Multiple choice (select one) Multiple choice (select many) Drop-down menu Multi-point scale (matrix single select) Matrix multi-select Smiley - Rating When I disable dynamic text/comment box, I get this warning message \"you've chosen to update/remove dynamic text/comments, however the system has already collected data.\" -- what does that mean? It means that the system has detected that you've tried to remove dynamic text/comment option from a question. However, the system also has prior collected data (while you were testing?) for that question. To avoid data corruption issues, you need to delete all the data collected. Information on how to delete data is listed below in the references section. Can I have the \"other\" option also with the dynamic comment option? Yes. The two of them are independent. Can I have more than one option to trigger the dynamic comment? Yes. For each of the items, make sure you choose the dynamic comment option where you want to trigger it. Can I enable dynamic comments on a multi-select (choose all that apply) question type or a matrix question with check boxes? Yes. We do support dynamic comments with multiple choice (choose all that apply) question types. Enabling dynamic comments will introduce another column in the export file. This column will store the open-ended text data. Please do not change the question type (e.g.: Radio Button » Checkbox) after enabling dynamic comments on your question. This is not supported and will result in data inconsistency. Can I use other logic functions along with the dynamic text option? Yes. All other logic functions are available when dynamic text boxes are used for a question. How many criteria can I add in a logic? You can add up to 5 criteria in a logic. Can I remove the validation from the dynamic comment box logic while keeping the validation on the question? No, however you can do this by adding multiple comment box questions and using show/hide logic to show or hide them based on the answers to the survey. Please contact our support team for further assistance. Dynamic comment box does not work when interactive mode is on.       "id": "extraction",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Extraction logic",
      "summary": "Extraction logic",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/extraction.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:51:02.889Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Extraction logic"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Extraction logic Extraction logic can be used when you want to ask a follow-up question or multiple follow-up questions based on the respondent's selection to a question in your survey. For example, consider a question in your survey \"Please select all smartphone brands that you have used in the past?\" with the following answer options: Apple Samsung Google Motorola OnePlus Xiaomi LG Other Based on the respondent's answer, you may want to ask follow-up questions for the selected brands. You can set this up easily using Extraction Logic How do I set up extraction logic? To set up extraction: Go to: Login » Surveys (click on a survey) » Edit. Click on Logic for the question from which you wish to extract follow-up questions. Select Extraction logic from the drop-down. Select which options you want to extract for the follow-up questions. You can select from the following options: Selected Choices: All options selected by respondents will be used in the extracted question. Not Selected Choices: All options that are not selected by respondents will be used in the extracted question. Displayed Choices: All options that are displayed to the respondent will be extracted. Not Displayed Choices: All options that are hidden by show/hide logic and not displayed to the respondent will be extracted. All Choices: All choices will be extracted. Click on the drop-down next to 'to question type' and select the question type for your follow-up question. Click on the Save Extraction Logic button. The extracted question will appear below the source question. You can add question text for your question. How does extraction logic work while answering the survey? In this example, respondents will be asked to select all the smartphone brands they have used. In the follow-up question, only the options selected by the respondents will be displayed. How do I extract the custom text entered in an “Other” option instead of the default label? Piping the “Other” option lets you pull the exact text instead of the default “Other” label. When someone selects the Other choice and writes their own answer, the system captures that custom text and shows it in the extracted question. Turn on the toggle, \"Pipe Text for Other Option\". Once enabled, the platform will auto-generate the extraction syntax for the Other text directly within the extracted question. Can I re-order the extracted question? The extracted question will be added immediately after the source question. If you are extracting multiple questions, the latest extracted question will be immediately after the source question. Once you have extracted questions, you can reorder the questions as per the order you want. Please see the Reorder Questions help article for more details. Why doesn’t the “Other (specify)” option change to a piped value when I enable “Pipe Text For Other Option” after saving extraction? This is expected. The “Pipe Text For Other Option” setting only affects options at the time the extracted question is first created. If you save the extraction with the toggle off, the “Other” choice is created as plain text and will not be updated automatically when you later turn the toggle on. To use piping, recreate the extraction with the toggle enabled before saving or manually edit the “Other” option text to add the piping expression. Deep dive into extraction logic | QuestionPro Webinar <span>Inline frames not supported</span> License This feature is available with the following licenses : Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Show or hide question logic",
      "summary": "Show or hide question logic",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/show-hide-question-logic.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:51:07.908Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Show or hide question logic"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Show or hide question logic Show/hide question logic, sometimes also referred to as display logic, is a feature that customizes a survey based on a respondent's answers or custom data. It automatically shows or hides specific questions, making the survey more relevant to each person. Using show/hide logic can greatly improve your survey's effectiveness. Better respondent experience: By hiding irrelevant questions, you keep your survey focused and reduce frustration. For instance, if you ask \"Do you own a pet?\" and the person answers \"No,\" all follow-up questions about pet food or vet visits will be hidden. Higher completion rates: When a survey is shorter and more personalized, respondents are less likely to get tired of answering questions they don't care about, which leads to more completed surveys. Higher-quality data: By tailoring questions to each respondent's specific situation, you gather more accurate and relevant data. This helps reduce response bias and gives you better insights. How do I set up show/hide question logic? Let's consider an example where we are asking respondents to select the phone brands they have used. For each phone brands, we have follow up questions that we want respondents to answer. However, it doesn't make sense to ask respondents questions about a brand that they have not used. To set this up, we can use show/hide question logic. Go to: Login » Surveys (select survey) » Edit For the question you want to show/hide, click on the logic option. On the Logic popup, select the Show/Hide Question logic option. Now, select whether to show or hide the question by default. In this example, we will hide the question by default. Now, name your criteria and set up the conditions: Once you have created the criteria, Save Logic When answering the survey, this question will now be hidden by default. Only if the respondent has selected the brand Apple in the earlier question will this question be shown to the respondent. What kind of logic can I use to show or hide questions? For show/hide question logic, you can create criteria based on the following: Questions: Logic criteria based on answers to questions in your survey. System variables: Logic criteria based on system variables updated on the survey or passed via URL or tracked survey links. Geo location: Based on country, region picked via IP address of respondents. Email lists: Based on email lists used to invite respondents to the survey. Device type: Based on the device respondents are answering their survey from. How does the dynamic (on page) logic toggle work? For show/hide question logic based on questions earlier in the survey, a page break is required before the question that needs to be hidden or shown. If you wish to show or hide questions dynamically on page, then you can enable the Dynamic (on page) logic toggle. With this toggle enabled, now you can show or hide questions on the same page as and how respondents answer questions on that page.       "content": "Scoring logic QuestionPro allows you to turn a survey into a quiz/online test by applying scoring logic to the questions. You can compute scores in real-time and present them to the respondents immediately. How do I apply scoring logic to the questions? To set up scoring logic: Go to: Login » Surveys (click on a survey) » Edit. Add all the required questions to your survey. Hover over the question on which you wish to apply logic and click on logic. A pop-up will appear. Click on Scoring. Set the desired score value for the correct answer option(s). You can also define a branching destination based on the qualified score under Branching on score. For example, consider that the respondents who score 10 points for a question are qualified to go to the next question and those respondents who score less than 5 points are not qualified to take the quiz further. In the \"if statement\", select question score or total score from the drop-down and set the range of qualified score. Select the destination for the qualified score by selecting an option from \"jump to\" drop-down. You can also add multiple if statements by clicking on plus (+). Click on Save Logic. Can I \"subtract\" or put in \"negative\" values for scores when respondent chooses incorrect answers? Yes. Simply enter in a negative value for the answer option. If the respondent chooses that option, the value will be subtracted from the total score. What question types are integrated with Scoring? Currently, scoring is available for Multiple choice select one type questions. Multiple choice select many type questions. Matrix question types. You can set a maximum score value for multiple choice select many type questions. Can I display the computed score to the end-user? Yes. You can display the computed score to the respondents on the \"Thank You Page\". Go to: Login » Surveys » Edit » Finish Options. Select the finish option as \"Thank You Page\". In the message, enter ${score} in the text area. Click on Save Changes. Can I store the score in a custom variable? Yes. You can store the score in a custom variable. Go to: Login » Surveys (click on a survey) » Edit. Add all the required questions to your survey. Hover over the question on which you wish to apply logic and click on logic. A pop-up will appear. Go to Scoring to the set desired score for the correct answer options. Click on the drop-down for Store section score in. Select a custom variable (e.g. custom variable 1) in which you want to store the score. Click on Save Logic. Can I (as the administrator) view the computed score for an individual? Go to: Login » Surveys » Analytics » Responses Click on the Response ID to view individual report. In the popup, you can view the score.       "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Net promoter scoring model",
      "summary": "Net Promoter Score - Scoring Logic",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/net-promotor-custom-scoring-model.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:51:33.944Z",

          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Net Promoter Score - Scoring Logic"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Net Promoter Score - Scoring Logic Net Promoter Score (NPS): The NPS is a scoring model developed by Bain & Co. consultant Frederick F. Reichheld. It is a widely-used model for measuring customer loyalty based on their inclination to tell others about their experience with the company or product. “Net Promoter” is a registered trademark of Satmetrix Systems. How does it work? Respondents respond on a 0 -to- 10 point rating scale question: “How likely are you to recommend Company/Product to your friend/colleague?” The scale is then divided into three sections: Promoters – Users who gave a 9 or a 10 Passives – Users who gave a 7 or 8 Detractors – Users who gave a 6 or lower Where can I select the Scoring Model and see the Reports? If you have included a Net Promoter Score question in your questionnaire, the scoring model will automatically be applied in the reports. However, the scoring model itself can also be applied to any Select One question type. Login >> Surveys >> Analytics >> Reports >> Dashboard From the Dashboard, select the question to which you want to apply the NPS scoring model. Click on the little gear icon in the upper right corner of the question window. Select Custom Scoring Model from the left navigation. In the Scoring Model drop-down, select Net Promoter Score. Select the level you want to apply to each answer option, Promoter, Passive, or Detractor. Click Save Scoring Model Values. The report for the question will now update with the NPS scoring model applied.       "id": "piping-text",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Piping text",
      "summary": "Piping text",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/piping-text.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:51:43.902Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Piping text"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Piping text Piping text is a way to pass custom text to the next question based on the option selected. Using piping text, you can ask follow-up questions and maintain context for respondents when they are responding or giving feedback. How do I set up piping through skip logic? Here we will look at piping text using the ${piping_text} replacement tag. A good example for using the ${piping_text} replacement tag is when you want to ask follow up questions for all options selected. This is also referred to as looping. Consider an example where you are asking respondents to select restaurants from which they have ordered pizzas and then ask them follow up questions for all selections. To set up piping logic with looping, go to: Login » Surveys (Select a survey) » Edit First set up your survey questions. Then click on the logic option to set up piping logic for the question we want to pipe text from. Under Skip Logic, enable looping logic. Now select the jump to question for each option and enter the required piping text. Click on Save Logic When respondents answer the survey, for the selected options, the piping text entered for the selected options will be used in the follow-up question Wherever you use the replacement tag ${piping_text}, it will be replaced on the following page. To pipe text to multiple pages, you can use piping through question code. How do I set up piping through question code? If you only want to display the option text that has been selected without modifications. Simply add the tag ${QUESTION_CODE} A few examples to help: If you want to pipe the option selected in question Q2 then the tag will be ${Q2}. If you want to pipe the option selected in question Demo2 then the tag will be ${Demo2}. If you want to pipe the other option for a question, then the tag will be ${Q2_OTHER}. Can I pipe text captured along with the 'Other' option for a question? Yes. You can pipe text captured along with the other option for a question. Format for piping other option text is: ${question_code_OTHER} For example, you want to pipe text captured with the other option for a question with code: Q5, then the format will be: ${Q5_OTHER}. Wherever you use this tag, the other option text will be displayed. Which question types support the use of piping text? Piping Text using the question code feature is supported by the following question types: Multiple Choice Text Graphical Rating (Except Text Slider) Matrix Table Ordering Image Chooser Calendar Customer Satisfaction Lookup Table Multi Tier Lookup Table Video Rating Stop Watch Barcode QR Code Reader Note: Piping using the question code feature is not supported in QuestionPro Offline App. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful? This can be helpful if you have a complicated set of questions that would make more sense to keep as separate surveys for reporting purposes, or even just to keep logic easy to track. How do I set up a chain survey? Before adding any logic, you should set up the entire survey with all of the questions. Select the survey for chaining from the drop-down, e.g. Customer Feedback Survey. Click on Save Logic. Note: When logic is set up for a question, a page break is automatically inserted. This page break is necessary for branching to function properly. If I chain from one survey to the other, can the data be automatically interlinked? Yes. The response ID of the first survey from where the respondent is directed to another survey is passed in as an external reference variable. This variable is available with the response for the chained survey. Along with the external reference variable, you can also pass up to 5 custom variables to the chained survey.       "id": "looping",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Looping logic",
      "summary": "Looping",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/looping.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:51:55.900Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Looping"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Looping When the same question or set of questions is asked multiple times for different options it is termed as looping. Looping allows you to dynamically \"loop\" through a set of questions based on responses to a multiple choice question. When do I use looping? Consider a scenario where you want to ask the following questions to your respondents: Q1. Please select the restaurants from which you have ordered pizzas. Pizza Hut Dominos Papa John's US Pizza Q2. How would you rate the pizza from <restaurant_name>? Poor Below average Average Good Excellent In this case, Q2 is the follow-up question of Q1 which will appear when the respondent selects answer options in Q1. The Q2 will keep appearing in a loop depending upon the number of options selected in Q1 by the respondent. If the respondent selects two options, then Q2 will appear twice. Important: Looping is available only for Multiple Choice Multiple Select (Check Box) type questions. Enabling Looping will ALWAYS PRODUCE MULTIPLE ROWS for the same response ID - One row for each loop. If you are planning on exporting the data to other statistical packages, please make sure that the other packages are able to handle the data in this format. SPSS does NOT handle the data with multiple rows for each loop. Cross-tabulation is not supported with looping due to the multiple rows of data. Surveys with looping logic should be answered in one go. Save and continue or save as you go is not supported with looping logic. How do I set up Looping? Before setting up looping, ensure that the rest of the survey is programmed and tested to avoid confusion when testing the loop. Since we want to replace the restaurant name in Q2, we need to use ${piping_text}. Piping text will be automatically replaced with the appropriate restaurant name for each loop. Select the question you want to apply looping logic to and click on logic. In the popup, with skip logic selected, switch the looping toggle on. For each answer option, select the question you would like to loop from the dropdown. Add piping text which should be shown in the next question which will be looped.       "content": "Selected N of M logic This feature will allow you to condition whether a question is displayed or not based on the number of options chosen by the respondent on a previous “Multiple choice: select many” question type. How do I setup \"Selected N of M logic? Consider this example below: Q1: Of the following, please select all products that you have bought in the last year. Selected N of M logic should be applied on Q1 to check if at least 3 products are selected. If yes, display Q2 and rest of the survey. Q2: Which among the following sentiment do you most associate with our brand? If no, terminate survey. To apply this logic, Go to: Login » Surveys (select a survey) » Edit. Select the question you wish to apply logic and click on logic. Select Compound Branching from the drop down. Enter a new criteria name. Select the question. By default, 'is' comparison operator is selected, here, select the selected comparison operator We want to set logic to check at least 3 or more options are selected. So select the at least option and enter the number of options to check. Set the jump to condition to go to Q2. Also set the skip condition in case the criterion is not met to get to the termination or thank you page. Click on Save to apply this logic.       "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "JavaScript Logic Syntax Reference"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "JavaScript Logic Syntax Reference What is JavaScript logic and how to use it? Link Copied QuestionPro's JavaScript logic facilitates the users to apply custom validation, special effects on the answer selection when the respondent is answering the surveys, etc. which enables the users to use their JavaScript knowledge and customize the survey to enhance the respondent's experience of answering the survey. To apply the JavaScript Logic to your surveys please follow the steps below: Go to: Survey >> Edit >> Workspace Click on the Logic button on the top right corner of the question Select the JavaScript Logic option from the first drop-down. Enable the toggle for Pre JavaScript Logic to apply the JavaScript logic before that specific question appears to the respondent or enable the toggle for Post JavaScript Logic to apply the JavaScript logic after the respondent has answered that specific question. Add the JavaScript inside the respective area and click on the Save Logic button. Test out the logic by answering the survey and make changes to the logic accordingly. What is the syntax of the default JavaScript functions provided by QuestionPro? Link Copied Please refer to the following table for the default syntax of the JavaScript functions provided by QuestionPro: Sr. No. JavaScript Function Explanation 1 $survey.getLanguageID(); This function will return the selected language id by the respondent while answering the survey. 2 $survey.getSelectedCount('Question-code'); This function will return number of options selected for multiple-choice question. Example: $survey.getSelectedCount('Q1'); will return the number of answer options selected by the respondent for multiple-choice question with question code Q1. 3 $survey.getSelectedOption('Question-code'); This function will return selected answer option's text(answer value) for multiple-choice question. Example: $survey.getSelectedOption('Q1'); will return the text(answer value) of the answer option selected by the respondent for question with code Q1. 4 $survey.getSelectedOptions('Question-code'); This function will return array of selected answer options for multiple-choice question. Example: $survey.getSelectedOption('Q1'); will return the array of answer options selected by the respondent for question with code Q1. 5 $survey.getSelectedOptionIndex('Question-code'); This function will return the index of the selected answer option for multiple-choice question. Example: $survey.getSelectedOptionIndex('Q1'); will return the index of the answer option selected by the respondent for question with code Q1. 6 $survey.getSelectedOptions('Question-code'); This function will return array of selected answer options for multiple-choice question. Example: $survey.getSelectedOption('Q1') will return the array of answer options selected by the respondent for question with code Q1. Additionally you can use this function with the multiple-choice select many question type by using the following syntax: $survey.getSelectedOptionIndex('Q1_1'); //for first row $survey.getSelectedOptionIndex('Q1_2'); // for second row 7 $survey.getCXTransaction(); This function will return the transaction details of the respondent. Transaction JSON: \"cxTransaction\": { \"emailAddress\": \"transaction@questionpro.com\", \"product\": \"product\", \"phoneNumber\": \"999999999\", \"productGroup\": \"product group\", \"cxDate\": \"2022-04-11\", \"languageID\": 247, \"language\": \"English\", \"Segment\": { \"parentBusinessUnitID\", \"comparatorBusinessUnitId\", \"country\", \"zipCode\", \"CXFeedbackID\", \"enableComparison\", \"externalRefID\", \"city\", \"isLeafSegment\", \"lowerLimit\", \"type\", \"deviceKeyID\", \"userCount\", \"name\", \"businessUnitCode\", \"overallStats\", \"addressLine1\", \"addressLine2\", \"upperLimit\", \"state\", \"primarySupervisorID\", \"timestamp\" } } 8 $survey.getCXTransaction().getSegment(); This function will return the segment details of the respondent. Segment JSON: \"Segment\": { \"parentBusinessUnitID\", \"comparatorBusinessUnitId\", \"country\", \"zipCode\", \"CXFeedbackID\", \"enableComparison\", \"externalRefID\", \"city\", \"isLeafSegment\", \"lowerLimit\", \"type\", \"deviceKeyID\", \"userCount\", \"name\", \"businessUnitCode\", \"overallStats\", \"addressLine1\", \"addressLine2\", \"upperLimit\", \"state\", \"primarySupervisorID\", \"timestamp\" } 9 $survey.getCXTransaction().getSegment().getCustomField('customFieldName'); This function will return the custom-field value from the segment. Note: Please replace the Question-code with actual code of the question on which you want to execute the JavaScript. Note: Points 7, 8, and 9 are only applicable for CX surveys.       "id": "block-flow",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Block Flow",
      "summary": "This helpfile explains the detailed step to view and edit survey block flow.",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/block-flow.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:52:29.891Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Block Flow"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Block Flow What is block flow? Link Copied The block flow is where you can view the order/flow of the blocks of questions present in your survey, rearrange them, add complex logic to them, and apply randomizations on them. The block flow is basically a one-stop location to decide the entire flow of your survey to be presented to the respondents based on their answers to the initial questions or their geo-location, device type, etc. which directly reduces the chances of order-bias and helps you get valid data for your research. How to access the block flow for a survey? Link Copied To view/edit the block flow for your survey, please follow the steps below: Go to: Survey >> Edit Click on the Block Flow option from the drop-down menu named Tools located at the top-right corner of the window. In the pop-up, you'll be able to view all the blocks present in your survey. The order of the blocks visible in the pop-up is the current flow/order of your survey. Now, you can: Drag & drop the blocks to re-arrange the flow/order of the survey. Click on Add Logic to add criteria-based logic at any point of the block flow. Click on Add Randomizer to add a new set of block randomizer at any point of the block flow. How can I add logic on the blocks? Link Copied To add logic on the survey blocks, please follow the steps below: Go to: Survey >> Edit. Click on Block Flow from the Tools drop-down menu located at the top-right corner of the window. In the pop-up, click on Add Logic within any two blocks. A new section will be added for adding the blocks under that specific logic. Drag & drop the blocks you wish to add under the new logic. Now, select the criteria on the top of the new section to display the blocks to the respondents. Click on the Save button. How to set up a block randomizer? Link Copied To set up a block randomizer on your survey, please follow the steps below: Go to: Survey >> Edit. Click on Block Flow from the Tools drop-down menu located at the top-right corner of the window. In the pop-up, click on Add Randomizer within any two blocks. A new section will be added for adding the blocks under that randomizer. Drag & drop the blocks you wish to add under the randomizer. Select the number of blocks you want to randomly display from the following list of blocks. Click on the Save button. Evenly present blocks logic toggle has been deprecated as of September 30th, 2024. How can I add a block randomizer based on a criteria-based logic? Link Copied To add a block randomizer based on a criteria-based logic, please follow the steps below: Go to: Survey >> Edit. Click on Block Flow from the Tools drop-down menu located at the top-right corner of the window. In the pop-up, click on Add Logic within any two blocks. A new section will be added for adding the blocks under that specific logic. Drag & drop the blocks you wish to add under the new logic. Now, select the criteria on the top of the new section to display the blocks to the respondents. Click on Add Randomizer on any block present inside the new logic section, to add a block randomizer within the logic. Drag & drop the blocks you want to randomize inside the new logic. Select the number of blocks you want to randomly display to the respondents. Click on the Save button. FAQs: Link Copied Can I export the block flow setup to share with my colleagues? Yes. You can export the block flow setup in a PDF file to share with other users and get their opinions on the setup by just clicking the PDF icon on the top-right corner of the block flow pop-up. I have too many blocks within my survey, and I am not able to access all the blocks under block flow. What can I do? If you have multiple blocks within the survey and are not able to view all the blocks in the pop-up, then click on the - icon on the top-right corner of the pop-up to adjust the zoom out a bit and access all the blocks. You can use the + icon to zoom back in and the Reset button to reset the zoom level to default. Can I add multiple logics under the block randomizer? Yes. You can add multiple logics under block randomizer or a logic section which will form a nested structure of logics & randomizers to completely eliminate the chances of order bias and help you get genuine responses to your research survey. License This feature is available with the following license : Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.",

      "content": "Block Looping What is block looping? Link Copied <span>Inline frames not supported</span> Looping helps you to dynamically show a block of questions multiple times to the same respondent. Example: A market research company wants to ask the same set of questions for different products a respondent uses. Instead of spending time, adding the same questions for each of the products, the questions can be added once. Looping can be used to repeat and display the set of questions for each individual product that the respondent has used.. How can I set up looping on a block in my QuestionPro survey? Link Copied To set up looping on your survey block, please follow the steps below: Go to: Survey >> Edit >> Workspace and add the survey blocks and questions according to your requirement. Click the menu icon on the top-right corner of the block and then click on the Looping option from the drop-down menu. In the pop-up, turn the Looping for Block toggle ON. Select the loop source from one of the following: Looping Directly: To loop the questions in the block for specified number of times. Looping based on question: To loop the questions in the block for the selected/ unselected displayed/ not displayed/ all choices of the loop source question. Add the piping text you want to display in the question/ answer text for each iteration and click on the Save button. Now test out the survey by using the anonymous/preview survey link to check the looping. In the example displayed in the screenshots, the survey experience will be: Select the answer options from the looping source questions You can see the piping texts for the first loop (selected choice in the source- Audi): Then you can see the piping texts for the second loop (selected choice in the source- Jeep): Similarly, you can add as many piping texts you need to diplay to respondents based on your survey requirement.       "id": "scoring-engine-syntax-reference",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Scoring Engine: Syntax Reference",
      "summary": "Scoring Engine Syntax Reference",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/scoring-engine-syntax-reference.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T10:52:47.910Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Scoring engine syntax reference"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Scoring engine syntax reference Note: The custom logic/scoring engine is available as a reference and in most cases requires programming from our professional service team. QuestionPro technical support cannot answer questions related to custom scripting. System Input Variables Description ${responseID} Unique Identifier for each response ${custom1} External Data Interface 1 ${custom2} External Data Interface 2 ${custom3} External Data Interface 3 ${custom4} External Data Interface 4 Dynamic Survey Variables ${<QUESTION_CODE>} This is the response value for the question with the corresponding question code. ${<QUESTION_CODE>_piping_text>} If you have Piping_Text enabled typically used for branching, then the value of the piped text is assigned to this variable. Computed Variables ${score} Computed score, also saved along with the results $survey.isDuplicateEntry(\"<QUESTION_CODE>\", \"test\") Assuming <QUESTION_CODE> is a text input question, isDuplicateEntry will return a true if any previous respondent has entered \"test\" $survey.extractAnswers(\"<QUESTION_CODE>\", [1,2,3,4]) Displays question extracted from <QUESTION_CODE> as if answer options 1, 2, 3 and 4 were selected. $survey.branchTo(\"<QUESTION_CODE>\") Branch to a Question based on Logic $survey.branchTo(\"<QUESTION_CODE>\", \"Piping Text\") Branch to a Question based on logic, and pipe the text into ${piping_text} $survey.branchTo(\"Thank_You_Page\") Branch to the Thank You page based on logic and the response is marked as completed. $survey.branchTo(\"Terminate_Page\") Branch to the Terminate page based on logic and the response is marked as terminated. $survey.sumValues() Adds all the values for all analytical (multiple choice, matrix, rating etc.) questions. $survey.sumValues(\"<QUESTION_CODE>\") Adds all the values for the question code. survey.sumValuesWithPrefix(String prefixCode) Adds all the values for all analytical (multiple choice) questions with a question code prefix. For example calling sumValuesWithPrefix(\"Q\") will sum all the questions with question codes that start with a \"Q\" survey.questionCountWithPrefix(String prefixCode) Counts the number of questions with that prefix code. This is meant to be used with the sumValuesWithPrefix -- This only accounts questions that have been answered and the question code starts with the prefix. $survey.computeAverage(\"<QUESTION_CODE>\") Computes the average values for the question code. $survey.chainTo(<survey_id>) Chains to another survey Conjoint Variable Syntax $CONJOINT.get(0) Gets the Reference to the first attribute, replace 0 with 1 to get the second attribute etc. $CONJOINT.get(0).getLevelAt(0) Gets the reference the first Level (within the first attribute) $CONJOINT.get(0).getLevelAt(0).getAverageUtility() Gets the value for the utility score computed for that user/response - for the first level within the first attribute $CONJOINT.get(0).getRelativeImportance() Gets the relative importance score for the first attribute Survey Logic/Branching Syntax $survey.branchTo(\"Q10\") Branches to question with question code = Q10 $survey.chainTo(23451) Branches to survey with ID = 23451 Analysis $survey.getCustomScore(<QUESTION_CODE>) Gets the \"AVERAGE\" value of the Custom Score for the question with the specific Code $survey.getCustomScore(<QUESTION_CODE>, <DATA_SEGMENT_CODE>) Gets the \"AVERAGE\" value of the custom score for the question with the specificcCode for a particular data segment $survey.getResponseCount(<QUESTION_CODE>, <ANSWER_INDEX>, <RESPONSE_STATUS>) Gets the number of respondents who have responded to the question identified with <QUESTION_CODE> with the answer identified with <ANSWER_INDEX> Charting $survey.drawCustomChart(<QUESTION_CODE>, <DATA_SEGMENT_CODE>, max) Draw's a horizontal bar graph for the given question, and data segment. $survey.drawCustomChart(<QUESTION_CODE>, <DATA_SEGMENT_CODE>, max, colorIndex) Draw's a horizontal bar graph for the given question, and data segment. The colorIndex (0-9) specifies the color to use for displaying the chart. $survey.drawSpotlightChart(<QUESTION_CODE>, <DATA_SEGMENT_CODE>, max, colorIndex) Draw's a horizontal bar graph for the given question with spotlight reporting, and data segment. The colorIndex (0-9) specifies the color to use for displaying the chart. Checking if an open-ended text is answered or not and then branching based on that: Let us assume that there is an open-ended text question with the question code : Q1. #if (${Q1_1}) $survey.branchTo(\"Q10\") #end ${Q1_1} - Represents the first open-ended text box in the question $survey.branchTo(\"Q10\") - Represents the branching directive Custom1-5 and External Reference Data Population $survey.updateCustom1(\"Value\") Updates the ${custom1} [Custom Variable 1] variable with the value $survey.updateExternalReference(\"Value\") Updates the ${ext_ref} [External Reference] variable with the value Custom6 and above. Up to 255 $survey.updateHighCustomVariable(6, \"Value\") Updates the ${custom6} variable with the value Summing all the values for the Multiple Choice/Ratings Questions #set ($score = $survey.sumValues()) The sumValues() method calculates the sum and assigns to the $score variable. Chaining to multiple surveys $survey.chainTo(3342) In the example above, 3342 is the Survey ID you want to chain to.
