      "content": "Import External Data Into New Survey To import External Data: Go to: My Surveys (Select Survey) » Analytics » Manage Data » Import Click on Sample Excel Document Fill the data as per the template Browse the file and click on Upload Data Do I need to create a survey for importing data? No. When you import data, a new survey is created automatically. All questions and answers are also created as per the imported file format. Can I edit the survey once I have imported data? Yes. You can edit the survey, change the text for questions and answers. However, we recommend that you don't make any structural changes. If you make structural changes, the data may get affected. I have imported data. I need to upload more responses/data to the same survey. Every time you import data, a new survey is created and the data is imported into the new survey. Data cannot be imported into an existing survey even if the survey was created importing data in the same format. Please maintain the data file that you used to import data offline. To add more responses, please add data to the same file and import the data again. A new survey will be created that will have the entire data. What should be the data format? Data can be imported from an excel (.xls) file format. The first eight columns are reserved for system variables. Actual survey questions start from the 9th column (I) onwards. First Column (A): External Reference Variable column (Optional) Second Column (B): Weight column (Optional) Third Column (C): Email Address (Optional) Fourth Column (D): Custom Variable 1 (Optional) Fifth Column (E): Custom Variable 2 (Optional) Sixth Column (F): Custom Variable 3 (Optional) Seventh Column (G): Custom Variable 4 (Optional) Eighth Column (H): Custom Variable 5 (Optional) Ninth Column (I): Question Type | Text (Required) - Actual Questions start from the ninth column onwards. Data for responses should start from the 3rd row in the Excel file. Currently there is a limit of 255 columns when importing data. Which are the supported question types? Below is the list of supported question types for \"Import External Data Into Existing Survey\" Multiple Choice: Select One, Select Many, Drop Down Menu Text: Comment Box, Single Row Text, Numeric Input, Email Address, Contact Information Graphical Rating: Star rating, Text Slider, Numeric Slider, Thumbs UP/Down, Smiley-Rating Matrix Table: Multi-Point Scales, Checkbox/Multi-Select, Spreadsheet, Side-By-Side Matrix Ordering: Drag and Drop Misc: Date/Time, Calendar Customer Satisfaction: Net Promoter Score Data/Reference: Lookup Table License This feature is available with the following licenses : Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "content": "Delete Survey and Data - How to? QuestionPro platform provides you an option to delete the data of a survey or delete the entire survey. Deleting survey responses will cater your need to repurpose the survey whereas deleting survey will help you keep your dashboard clean and up to date with the latest surveys. Lets have a look how you can delete the survey response and delete the survey. How can I delete a survey? To delete a survey, go to: Login » Surveys Depending upon your screen resolution, you will see the delete option on your screen or under the three dots - more options. For smaller screen/resolution, click on the three-dots more options and click on the Delete option. For larger screen/resolution, delete option will be on screen, click on the delete option to delete your survey. Click on the Delete icon for the survey. You can also select multiple surveys at a time and delete them. On the confirmation popup, click Yes to delete or cancel to not delete. Deleted surveys will be moved to the recycle bin. How can I delete data/responses for a survey? To clear the results and delete all responses for a survey, go to: My Surveys (Select Survey) » Analytics Click on the Manage Data menu under Analytics. Select the Delete option in the Manage Data drop down. To delete data, you have two options available here. Clear All Responses: Clicking on Clear Responses here will clear all data/responses. This action cannot be undone. Clear All Test Responses: Clicking on Clear Responses for this option will only delete responses that were collected using the Preview link for the survey. These responses are marked as test_response when saving. Click on Clear Responses to delete data: All response data will be deleted If you are using attach/upload file question, all files associated with the responses will also be deleted. If data was collected using our offline/mobile app, device audit data for the responses will also be deleted. Data once deleted cannot be retrieved! Why would I want to clear the data? If you are doing pre-test trials, etc., you usually want to clear out all the test data from the database before starting to collect \"real\" data. Use the \"Clear Data\" function for this.       ],
      "content": "Report Scheduler / Data Scheduler - How to? The Report Scheduler is a recurring and automated interface to automatically email you reports on a periodic basis. Typically this is used in a long running survey (transactional surveys) where you have a continuous data collection stream. On one hand, you have data being collected on an on-going basis, and on the other hand, you can create a daily, weekly or monthly schedule so that the data collected can automatically be emailed to your account. The Report Scheduler is meant for automatic delivery of the various reports that QuestionPro generates. How do I get started? Go to: My Surveys (Select Survey) » Analytics » Manage Data » Scheduler What are the different options for creating the Report Scheduler? Name: This is the name that identifies this particular scheduler event. Please make this as descriptive as possible - Example - Weekly Excel Report etc. Report: This is the kind of report that can be generated. Choose the best report that suits your needs. Frequency: How often do you want the report emailed to you automatically. Time: At what time do you want the report to be generated and delivered to you. End Date: After setting up the End date the schedule reports will automatically stop sending out reports after that scheduled end date. Data Window: By default, the reports will take into account all the data that has been collected under your account. However, in most cases, running the report on the entire data-set is not needed. It makes sense to generate the report only on the data collected say in the last three months or so. In cases where you only want to see the open-ended comments for a particular survey or simply see the scores generated in the last week, you can shorten the Data Window to be 1 week. Can I create schedules with other frequencies - Fortnightly, quarterly, etc? No. Currently, we are releasing daily, weekly and monthly schedule options only. This may change in the future depending upon how many users ask for other frequency options. How will I get notified when the report is ready? The generated reports will be emailed to you based on the email address under your User Profile. Can multiple users be notified automatically when reports are generated? Yes, with global multi-user accounts (available with the Enterprise License) you can choose the users within your organization that can receive the reports. Can I \"modify\" a schedule? At this point, we do not allow modifications to the scheduled events. You can, however, delete the events as necessary and schedule a new event. Can I temporarily suspend the scheduled reports? Yes. Just click on the Suspend icon. When the scheduler is Suspended, you will not receive any reports. What are the different kinds of report I can schedule? The following reports can be scheduled: Excel : Raw Data + Analytics This is the exact same Excel report that is generated by going to: Login » Surveys » Manage Data » Raw Data Export » MS Excel Report Excel : Trend Report This is the Trend Report that is generated by going to: Login » Surveys » Reports» Advanced » Trend Analysis SPSS : Data Collection Reporting This Data Collection Reporting can also be generated by going to: Login » Surveys » Manage Data » Statistical Package Export PowerPoint: This Overview Report can also be generated by going to: Login » Surveys » Manage Data » Export » Charts & Analytics Export CSV: Raw Data Login » Surveys » Manage Data » Raw Data Export » CSV Report HTML - Real-Time Text Report This is the Open-Ended Text report generated by going to : Login » Surveys » Reports » Open-Ended Text HTML - Real-Time Summary Report Custom Report : This Refresh Custom Report can also be generated by going to: Login » Surveys » Report » Custom » Customized Reports You can \"Refresh\" the data automatically by choosing this option. I have scheduled a report and I am not receiving it. What am I doing wrong? The scheduled reports are automatically emailed to the email address specified in your account. If you are not receiving the emails please check the following: SPAM/Junk Mail settings - Please check your email SPAM/Junk settings to make sure emails coming from QuestionPro are not categorized as SPAM/Junk. Please check with your IT department to make sure your organization is not blocking emails coming from QuestionPro as SPAM. You can verify that the report was run by clicking on the \"Next Schedule\" link for the scheduled report in question. Is there an upper limit for the number of report schedulers per survey? Yes, there is a limit as per the license level: Advanced - 3 Per Survey Team Edition - 3 Per Survey Enterprise - 5 Per Survey Research Edition - 5 Per Survey Daily frequency for report scheduler is only available with our Enterprise and Research Edition license.       "content": "Datapad for a survey What is datapad? Link Copied <span>Inline frames not supported</span> Datapad allows users to create subsets or snapshots of data collected from respondents that can be used directly in statistical tools like R studio and Jamovi for further analysis. Users can push back the analysis, charts and graphs back to the datapad and easily share the entire package using a public link. How do I create a datapad for a survey? Go to: Login » Surveys (Select Survey) » Analytics Under Dashboard, select Datapad option. Provide a title to the datapad and select the required fields. The system will auto generate a description for the datapad. Select an option to treat the missing values present in the dataset. Dataset summary will provide an overview of the data in the dataset. Click on the create dataset button to create a dataset. What are the different ways to treat the missing values in a datapad? We are providing 3 options under Impute missing values Include all values: This will keep the data in the raw form without any treatment. The datapad will have missing values which late user can treat themselves in R studio. Impute missing values with mean/mode: Here in case of numeric variables missing values are imputed with mean of the coloumn and in case of categorical variable the imputation is done with mode. This method of imputation does not reduce the number of responses. Exclude missing values from the datapad: Here we exclude the entire response(row) containing missing value from the datapad.In this case the number of responses reduces. How do I use the datapad created in R studio? Click on copy button to use the dataset created in R studio. URL of the created datapad gets copied. Install RStudio open source software in your machine. Write a command in the workspace as shown below and Run the command to obtain the datapad. Will the data in the datapad created gets updated realtime? No the data in the datapad will not get updated realtime. The concept here is to provide an ability to create a snapshot. If there are 30 responses and you create a dataset then even when the responses collected increase to 50 there will not be any change in the datapad created earlier with 30 responses. How many datapads can I create for a particular survey? You can create as many as datapads you want to create for a particular survey. Datapad once created can be reused for analysis whenever required. How do I access the list of all datapads created for a particular survey? Once you create a first datapad, again when you visit datapad page, you will directed to datapad list page where you can view all the datapads created.       "content": "Data Quality - Plagiarism Detection We have added plagiarism detection to our data quality checks. When plagiarism detection is enabled under data quality, we identify responses if they were submitted by bots like ChatGPT or other automated or AI solutions available. If a response is detected as submitted by bots, the response will be flagged and can also be terminated for data quality. We can detect text responses if they are pasted instead of typed in by respondents. These checks allow you to weed out bad responses and making sure that your data is clean and of the highest quality. Here is a quick glimpse of how plagiarism detection works How do I enable plagiarism detection for a survey? Go to: Login » Surveys (Select Survey) » Analytics » Manage Data Under Manage Data, select Data Quality option. Switch on the toggle for the Plagiarism Detection data quality flag. Responses collected after enabling selected modules will be screened for the quality checks. You can click on the settings link for plagiarism detection to switch on/off Copy/Paste Detection Bot Response Detection How is the response flagged for bot detection? We have our special sauce to score response against bot responses. Responses with similarity score above 80% match will be flagged for bot detection. How to terminate a response if a bot response is detected? When data quality flags are enabled, by default the responses are only flagged. If you wish, you can also terminate responses. You can combine multiple flags under terminate logic. To terminate responses for plagiarism detection flag, you can select it under the terminate response option. Where can I check flagged responses? Go to: Analytics » Responses Here you can view the flagged responses. Click on the response ID and the response will open in a popup.       "steps": [],
      "content": "IP based location data When your respondents answer any survey, their IP address is stored along with location data for additional insights. IP based location data tracking can be used to gate respondents based on logic or analyze data based on location. However, for privay protection and based on your needs, you can disable IP based location tracking for your account. How is IP based location tracking data collected and stored? When respondents answer any survey, their IP address is captured and stored along with their response. Based on their IP address, we use maxmind geo-coding to find out location data like, country, region, longitured, latitude, radius. This information is stored along with the response data for each response. You can view this data under responses and this data is also included when you export raw data for your survey. Go to: My Surveys (Select Survey) » Analytics » Dashboard » Responses Here you can view IP based location data. How do I switch off IP based location data tracking? By default, IP based location tracking is enabled for all accounts. To disable IP based location data collection and tracking, Go to: My Account (Top right corner) In the drop down, select Global Settings Under Survey Settings, switch off the toggle for IP based location tracking. Once you make this change, going forward, location data will not be collected or tracked for responses. IP based location tracking data already collected on responses cannot be removed. Once you disable IP based location data collection, only new responses collected will not have this data. This change will apply to all surveys in your account. When IP based location tracking is switched off, Maps on dashboard will be disabled. How do I switch off IP based location data tracking for my entire organization? To disable IP based location data collection for your organization, on the surveys list page, Go to: Organization » Settings and click on the Settings button. On the popup, scroll down to the IP based location tracking setting, uncheck the selection box and click on Save Changes Once you disable IP based location tracking for the organization, all new users will have this setting disabled for their account and surveys. If you have existing users, they will need to disable this setting under their account.       "content": "Export Data - SPSS File Download How can I export a SPSS file for my survey data? To download a SPSS file of your data, go to: My Surveys (Select Survey) » Analytics Under Manage Data click on the Export option. Scroll down to the Statistical Package Export (SPSS) section. Here you can select the required settings for the export and click on Download to export the file. The following settings or controls are available for customization when exporting to SPSS. Include Raw Data: You can choose to include raw data and select either Display Answer Codes/Index or Display Answer Values Legacy Export: Use this toggle to export SPSS file with an older file format. Question Code Variable Name: By default, auto-generated SPSS variable names are used when exporting data. Select this toggle to use question codes instead of auto-generated variable names. Click here to learn more about SPSS variable names Question Code instead of Text: Select this toggle if you want to Display Question Index Instead of Variable Name. By default, the question numbers appear as variables (var1, var2, etc.). Checking this box will replace var1 with Q1, var2 with Q2, etc. Show Question Not Displayed: Hidden questions will be exported using a Dot (.) representation. Unselected Checkboxes = 0: By default, check boxes that are not selected are blank. Selecting this toggle will display a 0 instead of blank. Report Labels: If you have long question or answer text, you can use report labels instead of the long text. Open-Ended Text Data: Open-ended text type data will be exported along with close-ended data. Geo Code & Additional Info: Select this toggle to export metadata collected along with respondents' feedback. Include Randomization Data: Enable this toggle if you wish to see randomization order if you are using block randomization. Language Data Filter: By default, all language responses will be exported. You can select if you wish to download data for particular language when using multilingual surveys. Display Language: Select the primary language for exporting the data. Questions and answers will be in the selected language. Data Filters: By default, the entire data is exported. You can choose specific filters for time or response status to export filtered data. In the SPSS file, we represent the unanswered options of multi-select question type with \".\" which is the standard representation in SPSS. For SPSS report, the limit is set to 10,000 characters for a comment box question type, and 500 characters for other open-ended question types. QuestionPro auto-generates variable names by default and you generally don't need to change these. SPSS variable names cannot include special characters Duplicate variable names are also not permitted in SPSS For older versions of SPSS (pre 12.0) there is a character limit of 8 Variable names cannot end with an underscore or a period Variable names with reserved words are not allowed Any changes made to question code in the SPSS variable name pop-up will be applicable only to SPSS variable name in the export file. Question codes for your survey questions will not be updated. How are question codes displayed instead of auto-generated variable names?         "Map a custom variable: To map a custom variable value of the response to a Dynamics field. Available custom variables are from custom3 to custom255.",
        "Map a system variable: To map the system variable values to a Dynamics field. Available system variables are ResponseID, Timestamp, Device Data, and Language.",

      ],
      "content": "Microsoft Dynamics CRM Integration The Microsoft Dynamics CRM integration with QuestionPro allows you to automatically send survey responses to your Dynamics CRM account. With the new OAuth-based authentication method, users can securely connect their Dynamics account by logging in through Microsoft's authorization flow—no need to store or share passwords. Once connected, you can map survey questions to CRM fields and define whether to create or update records in Dynamics (like Leads or Contacts). This ensures your CRM data is always up to date based on real-time feedback from surveys. How to use Microsoft Dynamics integration to connect the QuestionPro survey to your Microsoft Dynamics CRM account? To integrate your QuestionPro survey with your Microsoft Dynamics account, please follow the steps below: Go to: Survey >> Integration >> Microsoft Dynamics Enter the name of the connection and your Microsoft Dynamics organization URL. Then click the Save button. Click on the Login with MS Dynamics button. The OAuth page will open in a new window. Enter your Microsoft username and click on the Next button. Then enter your Microsoft password and click on the Sign in button. After creating a connection, you will need to map the survey to your Dynamics object. Click on the + Add New Mapping button to add the field mapping for the current survey. Select the action you want to perform on each new QuestionPro response from the first drop-down: Create New: To create a new record in the Dynamics object. Update Existing: To update an existing record in the Dynamics object. Update if exists, else create new: To avoid duplicate records in the Dynamics object. Select your Dynamics object in the second drop-down and click on the Continue button. In the next screen, select the mapping type for the current survey from the first drop-down: Map a question: To map the answer code of the question to a Dynamics field. Map a custom variable: To map a custom variable value of the response to a Dynamics field. Available custom variables are from custom3 to custom255. Map a system variable: To map the system variable values to a Dynamics field. Available system variables are ResponseID, Timestamp, Device Data, and Language. Map an answer value: To map the answer value to a Dynamics field. Pull Data: To pull existing data from a Dynamics record and use it within the survey. Select the question or variable from the QuestionPro survey in the second drop-down, and select the Dynamics object field in the third drop-down. Use the '+' and '-' buttons on the right side of each field mapping to add or delete options, respectively. Ensure you have provided mappings for all the required fields, then click on the Continue button. Note: Please make sure to collect the responses for your survey from the Survey URL provided inside the mapping to send the data to your Dynamics object. Otherwise, the data will not be sent to your Dynamics object; it will remain in your QuestionPro account. After configuring the mappings, you will see a list of mappings created for that survey. You can review all the mappings for that survey using the snapshot view by hovering over each mapping. You can click on the mapping or the Edit button in the snapshot view to edit that particular mapping. Once you answer the survey, you can find the response pushed to your Microsoft Dynamics CRM account as per the mappings: FAQs: Can I use any custom objects from my MS Dynamics CRM? Yes, you can integrate your QuestionPro survey with any of your custom MS Dynamics object. License This feature is available with the following license : Research Edition Was the article helpful?         "Pull Data: To pull existing data from Salesforce record and use it within the survey. (This is only available under Update Existing mapping)"
      ],
      "content": "Salesforce Integration How to use the new Salesforce integration to connect the QuestionPro survey to Salesforce? Please follow the steps below to connect your QuestionPro survey to Salesforce: Go to: Survey >> Integration >> Salesforce Enter the desired name of the connection and click on Save button. Note: You can switch ON the Sandbox toggle to connect to Salesforce Sandbox environment but Sandbox connection is an Enterprise Edition feature. Click on the Sign in with Salesforce button. The OAuth page will be opened up in the new window. Enter your Salesforce username and password and click on Login button. After creating a connection you will need to map the survey to your Salesforce object. Click on + Add New Mapping button to add the field mapping for the current survey. Select the action you want to perform on each new QuestionPro response from the first drop-down: Create New: To create new record in Salesforce object. Update Existing: To update existing record in Salesforce object. Update if exists, else create new: To avoid duplicate records in Salesforce object. Select your Salesforce object in the second drop-down and click on Continue button. In the next screen, select the mapping type for the current survey from the first drop-down: Map a question: To map the answer code of the question to Salesforce field. Map a custom variable: To map custom variable value of the response to Salesforce field. Available custom variables are from custom3 to custom 255. Map a system variable: To map the system variable values to the Salesforce field. Available system variables are ResponseID, Timestamp, Device Data, Language and Survey Name. Map an answer value: To map the answer value to the Salesforce field. Pull Data: To pull existing data from Salesforce record and use it within the survey. (This is only available under Update Existing mapping) Select the question or variable from the QuestionPro survey in the second drop-down, and select the Salesforce object field in the third drop-down. Use the '+' and '-' buttons on the right side of each field mapping to add or delete options, respectively. Ensure you have provided mappings for all the required fields, then click on the Continue button. Note: Please make sure to collect the responses for your survey from the Survey URL provided inside the mapping to send the data to your Salesforce object other wise the data will not be sent to your Salesforce object, it will remain in your QuestionPro account. After configuring the mappings you will be available with a list of mappings created for that survey. You can go through all the mappings for that survey by using the snapshot view by hovering over each mapping. You can click on the mapping or the Edit button on the snapshot view to edit that particular mapping. How to troubleshoot Salesforce integration? Please follow the steps below to troubleshoot your Salesforce integration with QuestionPro survey: Go to: Survey>>Integration>>Salesforce>>Your Mapping>>Logs Here you can view all the logs against each and every responseID along with their status and error message if any. Repost: This will allow you to fetch the data for the same responseID once you have rectified the error. Also, at the same time the system will update the status. Bulk Repost: Say for example you have received 100 responses but due to poor mapping you could not fetch the data. No worries you can now rectify the issue and perform repost in bulk by selecting all response ID. Search: You can search a particular response ID using the search engine. Filter: You can also filter the response based on their status as All, Success, Failed and Response Deleted. Can I get Email notification for failed response? Yes, user will be notified through email on the registered email address with all the details like, survey ID, Mapping name, response ID with error details. To avail this feature you will have to switch on the toggle button for Error Notifications. Note: There can be different errors that can occur due to various reasons. So you will be notified only once in a 24 hours for each unique error. Limits ResourceTeam EditionResearch Edition Licence No. of connections2100 No. of object mappings10100 No. of field mappingsUnlimitedUnlimited FAQs: Can I push the survey data to multiple objects in Salesforce? Yes. You can push the survey responses to multiple Salesforce objects. To achieve the same you'll have to, Keep all the mappings within the survey on \"Create New\" records mode Use the anonymous or tracked survey links instead of the survey link present inside the mappings Can I use Salesforce integration with shared surveys No, Salesforce integration is not available for shared surveys. Only the survey owner can integrate their survey with Salesforce. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "summary": "QuestionPro - MTurk Integration",

        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "QuestionPro - MTurk Integration"

      "steps": [],
      "content": "QuestionPro - MTurk Integration What is MTurk? Amazon Mechanical Turk (MTurk) is a crowdsourcing marketplace that makes it easier for individuals and businesses to outsource their processes and jobs to a distributed workforce who can perform these tasks virtually. This could include anything from conducting simple data validation and research to more subjective tasks like survey participation, content moderation, and more. MTurk enables companies to harness the collective intelligence, skills, and insights from a global workforce to streamline business processes, augment data collection and analysis, and accelerate machine learning development. Alternatives to MTruck for your respondent panel.- QuestionPro Audience .https://www.questionpro.com/audience/ Steps to connect QuestionPro to MTurk Step 1 Paste the survey link from QuestionPro on MTurk, you’ll want to start from the Survey Link template. This enables you to post a task (HIT) on MTurk that directs Workers to your survey. Step 2 You will be prompted to provide a name, description, and keywords for your survey, be as descriptive as possible and include an estimate of how long your survey will take. You can then specify how much you will pay for each response and the number of respondents (Workers) you want to participate in your survey. Step 3 Select Auto-Approval settings for your Project. This value tells MTurk how long to wait before automatically approving a Worker’s response. If you set this value too short, you may not leave yourself enough time to fully review Workers’ responses before they are automatically approved. If you set this value too long, Workers may become frustrated at the delay in receiving payment upon completion of the survey. Customers conducting surveys tend to find that three days is a good balance, but please adjust to suit your survey requirements. Tracking responses You can do this either by providing Workers with a unique completion code or collecting a Worker’s ID in the survey itself. A unique completion code when complete you can then direct Workers to provide it in the MTurk task when they are done. You can then link the survey response to the Worker in your results. Instructions for doing this with QuestionPro are below Assigning Random IDs to Respondents You may need to create a random ID for each of your respondents. This is useful if you are running a study with MTurk where you ask respondents to prove their completion with an ID. To generate a Random ID there are a few steps. First select Survey Options in the menu for your survey. Generating a Completion Code in QuestionPro To generate a unique completion code here are a few steps. Go to the question in your survey that asks Workers for their Worker ID or email address and click on logic: Step 2 Step 3 In order to Display the Embedded Data in a question click the question to edit it. Add custom code on the Thank you page, to show the unique code after completing the survey. Researchers can view a list of people who sign up for their study and are able to administer (or not administer) credit based on participation. The system also allows administrators to establish the number of credits needed for a participant to successfully complete their course study. Administrators and instructors can even go as far as granting special non-study credit to students who complete an alternative assignment, such as a research paper or advanced project. Managing credits allows users to: Have full control over credit allocation-assigning or indicating who did not attend Run participant reports and easily make assessment of participation performance Give non-study credits for participants who complete research alternatives See a list of students enrolled in their course and their credits SONA Research Management: Sona Systems is the leader in cloud-based participant pool management software, helping thousands of research coordinators and researchers around the world set up studies, recruit participants, and manage credits or paid participation. Not only does the software grant researchers the ability to prescreen participants, but also restrict eligibility based on past participation in other studies, and manage study schedules. Furthermore, the system produces a wide array of participant pool usage reports and configuration options, giving research coordinators tight control over the entire research process. Alternatives to SONA for your respondent panel.- QuestionPro Audience External Study Credit Granting with QuestionPro While these instructions refer to \"credit-granting\", they work for both credit and paid studies.       "content": "Copper CRM Integration What is Copper CRM? Copper CRM features extensive and tight integration with the Google Workspace suite of applications, filling a gap in the CRM market. As the only sales automation solution endorsed by Google, this makes Copper CRM an appealing choice for businesses that are Google workshops. Copper CRM is mostly used by small to medium sized businesses of 1–200 employees with up to $50M in revenue. The most common industries that use Copper CRM are computer software and information technology and services. To get your free account click here. How to integrate QuestionPro survey responses with Copper CRM? Please follow the steps below to connect your QuestionPro survey to Copper CRM: Go to: Survey » Integration » Copper Enter the desired name of the connection, provide your Copper API Key, and enter your Copper email address. Click on the Save button. After creating a connection you will need to map the survey to your Copper object. Click on + Add New Mapping button to add the field mapping for the current survey. Select the action you want to perform on each new QuestionPro response from the first drop-down: Create New: To create new record in Copper object. Update Existing: To update existing record in Copper object. Select your Copper object in the second drop-down and click on Continue button. In the next screen, select the mapping type for the current survey from the first drop-down: Map a question: To map the answer code of the question to Copper field. Map a custom variable: To map custom variable value of the response to Copper field. Available custom variables are from custom3 to custom 255. Map a system variable: To map the system variable values to the Copper field. Available system variables are ResponseID, Timestamp, Device Data and Language. Map an answer value: To map the answer value to the Copper field. Pull Data: To pull existing data from Copper record and use it within the survey. Select the question/ variable from QuestionPro survey from second drop-down and select the Copper object field from the third drop-down. Use the \"+\" and \"-\" buttons on the right side of each field mapping to add or delete new option respectively. Make sure you have provided mappings to all the required feilds then click on Continue button. Note: Please make sure to collect the responses for your survey from the Survey URL provided inside the mapping to send the data to your Copper object other wise a copy of the data will not be sent to your Copper object, it will remain in your QuestionPro account only. After configuring the mappings you will be available with a list of mappings created for that survey. You can go through all the mappings for that survey by using the snapshot view by hovering over each mapping. You can click on the mapping or the Edit button on the snapshot view to edit that particular mapping. After the setup is done, the integration will trigger once the respondents hit the Submit/Done button on the survey. After the survey completion you can see a new record created on your Copper CRM in case of \"Create New\" action. How to get API key from Copper CRM? To get the API key from Copper CRM, follow the steps below: Login to your Copper CRM and go to Settings » Integrations » API Keys. Click on the Generate API Key button. A new API key will be created for your account. You can add a label for the new API key. License This feature is available with the following license : Research Edition Was the article helpful? The data collected via a QuestionPro survey can be used to create new contacts or update existing contacts in Hubspot contacts in real time. How to use new HubSpot integration to connect the QuestionPro survey to your HubSpot account? Map a system variable: To map the system variable value to the HubSpot field. Available system variables are ResponseID, Timestamp, Device Data and Language. Map an answer value: To map the answer value of a question to the HubSpot field. Pull data: To pull existing data from HubSpot contact and use it within the survey. Note: The custom variables custom1 and custom2 are used to send response data to the corresponding records in HubSpot. To ensure the integration works as expected, please avoid using these variables in your survey logic or flow.       "content": "Tableau Connector The Tableau Connector allows you to connect your QuestionPro survey directly to Tableau Desktop to create interactive dashboards and visualizations without the need for manual data exports. With this connector you can: Pull survey response data directly into Tableau. Choose whether to import all responses or only completed responses. Select your preferred answer display type. Refresh your data anytime within Tableau. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video Prerequisites Before setting up the integration, ensure you have: An active QuestionPro account with license - Team Edition or above. An active Tableau Desktop license. Your QuestionPro API key and Survey ID. Knowledge of your QuestionPro data center (US, EU, CA, etc.). A stable internet connection to fetch data from QuestionPro. Permissions to install connectors on your system. How do I download the Tableau Connector TACO file? Log in to your QuestionPro account. Navigate to Integration tab. Click on Tableau integration tile. Click QuestionPro .taco file to download the file. Note : The QuestionPro .taco connector requires Tableau Desktop version 2019.4 or later. Installing the connector on Windows OS Download QuestionPro.taco. Open your file browser (File Explorer on Windows or Finder on macOS). Navigate to your Tableau My Tableau Repository: Windows: C:\\Users\\{YourUsername}\\Documents\\My Tableau Repository\\Connectors macOS: /Users/{YourUsername}/Documents/My Tableau Repository/Connectors replace {YourUsername} with your actual username. If the Connectors folder doesn’t exist, create it manually. Move QuestionPro.taco into the Connectors folder. Relaunch Tableau — you’ll now see the QuestionPro Connector in the Connect pane. How do I connect my QuestionPro survey to Tableau? To connect your QuestionPro survey to Tableau, follow the steps below: Open Tableau and click on More option in the left navigation menu. From the apps menu, select QuestionPro Connector by QuestionPro. Select your Data Center (US, EU, CA, etc.). Enter your Survey ID and API Key. Then click Proceed. On the next screen, choose your import preference. Response Type: Choose All Responses (includes Completed + Started) or Completed Responses Only. Answer Display Type: Select answer code or answer text. Header (Question) Display Type: Select question text or question code. Then click Fetch Data. The import process will start and you'll see the success message after the import is completed. Troubleshooting Can’t connect to QuestionPro? Verify your API Key is correct. Ensure you have selected the correct Data Center (US, EU, CA, etc.). Confirm your internet connection is stable. If using a corporate network, check if a firewall or proxy is blocking Tableau from connecting to QuestionPro servers. Restart Tableau after making any configuration changes. Missing data fields? Ensure the survey has at least one response, empty surveys will return no data. Check if you selected the correct Answer Display Type (coded values vs full text). Verify you have chosen the correct Response Type (completed responses only vs all responses). If changes were made to the survey structure, refresh the Tableau extract to sync fields. Connector not appearing in Tableau? Confirm the .taco file is in the correct Connectors folder for your OS. Ensure you are running a Tableau version that supports custom connectors. Data refresh is slow Filter the data within Tableau to reduce the volume of records being loaded. Use Completed Responses Only if partial data is not required. Ensure your network speed meets Tableau’s recommended bandwidth for live data connections. I am not able to see my QuestionPro API key, how do I generate the API key? If you cannot see you QuestionPro API key on the Tabelau Connector screen then please click here to learn about generating the API key. I am not sure on which datacenter(DC) I am on, how do I find my datacenter? Datacenter can be found under My Account dropdown. Click here to know more.       "id": "Prolific-Integration",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Prolific Integration",
      "summary": "Prolific Integration",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/Prolific-Integration.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:13:23.956Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Prolific Integration"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Prolific Integration QuestionPro integrates with Prolific, allowing researchers to recruit participants via Prolific and collect their responses in QuestionPro. Through this integration, participant identifiers are automatically captured, associated with the corresponding survey responses, and returned to Prolific once the survey is completed. How do I capture Prolific participant identifiers? Prolific automatically passes participant information to QuestionPro through URL parameters. To capture these, append the parameters to your Study URL in Prolific: In QuestionPro, create custom variables to store the values passed from Prolific. For example, map PROLIFIC_PID to custom1, STUDY_ID to custom2, and SESSION_ID to custom3. Add your QuestionPro survey URL as the study URL within Prolific. Append the parameters directly to your link. For example: https://www.questionpro.com/t/surveyURL?custom1={{%PROLIFIC_PID%}}&custom2={{%STUDY_ID%}}&custom3={{%SESSION_ID%}}. How do I store the participant identifiers from Prolific in the survey? Go to your survey, fetch the PROLIFIC_PID from the custom variable in the study link say custom1. This would prepopulate the prolific ID in the field. How to Redirect participants to the Completion URL at the end of your survey? After respondents complete the survey, they should be redirected back to Prolific so that their submission is recorded successfully. In your Prolific study settings, navigate to the Completion paths section and select Redirect URL. Copy the generated Completion URL. In QuestionPro, open your survey and navigate to Edit >> Settings >> Finish Options. Select Automatic Redirect. Paste the Prolific Completion URL and save your changes. How to redirect participants if there are multiple completion paths? If there are multiple completion paths, in the Finish Options, you can use Panel Integration feature to redirect participants to different URLs. For example, if a participants fails the attention check and the survey for them is terminated, you can add the attention check completion path in Terminates section.       "content": "[BETA] Power BI Connector The Power BI Connector allows you to connect your QuestionPro survey directly to Power BI Desktop to create interactive dashboards and visualizations without the need for manual data exports. With this connector you can: Pull survey response data directly into Power BI. Choose whether to import all responses or only completed responses. Select your preferred header display type. Select your preferred answer display type. Refresh your data anytime within Power BI. Prerequisites Before setting up the integration, ensure you have: An active QuestionPro account with license - Research Edition. An active Power BI Desktop license. Your QuestionPro API key and Survey ID. Knowledge of your QuestionPro data center (US, EU, CA, etc.). A stable internet connection to fetch data from QuestionPro. Permissions to install connectors on your system. How do I download the Power BI Connector MEZ file? Log in to your QuestionPro account. Navigate to Integration tab. Click on Power BI integration tile. Click QuestionPro.mez file to download the file. Note : The QuestionPro.mez connector requires Power BI Desktop version 2024 or later (2.128.xxxx.0 or later). Installing the connector on your device Download QuestionPro.mez. Open your file explorer on Windows. Navigate to your Power BI folder: C:\\Users\\{YourUsername}\\Documents\\Power BI Desktop\\Custom Connectors. Replace {YourUsername} with your actual username. If the Custom connectors folder doesn’t exist, create it manually. Move QuestionPro.mez into the Custom connectors folder. Relaunch Power BI — you’ll now see the QuestionPro Connector in the Connect pane. How do I connect my QuestionPro survey to Power BI? To connect your QuestionPro survey to Power BI, follow the steps below: Open Power BI and click on Get data from other sources. Search for QuestionPro, select the connector and then click on Connect. Enter your Data Center (US, EU, CA, etc.). Enter your Survey ID. Choose Response Type. Enter 0 for Completed responses only. Enter 1 for All responses (includes Completed + Started). Choose Answer Format. Enter 0 for answer code format. Enter 1 for answer text format. Choose Header (Question) Format. Enter 0 for question code format. Enter 1 for question text format. Then click OK. Enter your API key to proceed. The import process will start and you'll see an option to load data. Click on Load data to import data into Power BI. Troubleshooting Can’t connect to QuestionPro? Verify your API Key is correct. Ensure you have selected the correct Data Center (US, EU, CA, etc.). Confirm your internet connection is stable. If using a corporate network, check if a firewall or proxy is blocking Power BI from connecting to QuestionPro servers. Restart Power BI after making any configuration changes. Missing data fields? Ensure the survey has at least one response, empty surveys will return no data. Check if you selected the correct Answer Display Type (coded values vs full text). Verify you have chosen the correct Response Type (completed responses only vs all responses). If changes were made to the survey structure, refresh the Power BI extract to sync fields. Connector not appearing in Power BI? Confirm the QuestionPro.mez file is in the correct Custom connectors folder for your OS. Ensure you are running a Power BI version that supports custom connectors. Data refresh is slow Filter the data within Power BI to reduce the volume of records being loaded. Use Completed Responses Only if partial data is not required. Ensure your network speed meets Power BI’s recommended bandwidth for live data connections. I am not able to see my QuestionPro API key, how do I generate the API key? If you cannot see you QuestionPro API key on the Power BI Connector screen then please click here to learn about generating the API key. I am not sure on which datacenter(DC) I am on, how do I find my datacenter? Datacenter can be found under My Account dropdown. Click here to know more. Yes, QuestionPro Surveys platform do allow you to update the status of your API Key with the following process: Click to download video To update the status of your API key from your QuestionPro account, please follow the steps below: Go to: Login » Surveys » Integration » API Click on Inactive toggle and the API key will be marked as Inactive. Once the API key is marked as Inactive all the API calls executed with the Inactive API key will get the following error: Note:The API calls made with the Inactive API key will not be included in your API consumption. Link Copied Yes, QuestionPro provides free API calls with the subscription. Following table displays the free API calls limit for each license: Licence No. of API calls Advanced 5,000 Team Edition 10,000 Research Edition 20,000 Communities 20,000 Customer Experience 20,000 Workforce 20,000 University Site License 10,000 University Research Edition 20,000 You get 500 free API calls with each trial licence. The API calls listed above will be available during the subscription duration. What happens if I surpass the free API calls limit? It's important to be aware that once you have reached the free API limits provided with your subscription, the APIs will no longer be accessible. Any API calls made after the limit is exhausted will result in a 429 Too Many Requests HTTP status. To continue using the APIs, please get in touch with your account representative to explore purchasing additional API calls for your subscription. What elements of the survey data are available via the XML format? The XML Export data contains 2 general sections: The survey structure (Survey->Questions->Answers). The complete response of the respondent. How to set up XML API Real-Time Post? You can configure the XML API to send (POST) information in real-time to any URL of your choice. To set up the Real-Time Post for your survey please follow the steps below: Go To: Login >> Survey >> Integration. Click on the Real Time Post integration Switch the HTTP/XML Post toggle ON. Now whenever the respondent completes this survey the data will be automatically posted to the URL specified in XML format.       "content": "QuestionPro Zapier App What is Zapier Zapier is an online automation tool that connects your favorite apps, such as Gmail, Slack, Mailchimp, and more. You can connect two or more apps to automate repetitive tasks without coding or relying on developers to build the integration. The QuestionPro App on Zapier allows you to instantly connect QuestionPro with 2,000+ apps to automate your work and find productivity super powers. How to use QuestionPro app on Zapier? Log In to your Zapier account Click on Make a Zap! Select QuestionPro as your Trigger App and New Response as the trigger and click on Continue Connect your QuestionPro account to zapier by clicking on Connect an Account button In the new window enter your QuestionPro account's API Key and click on Yes, Continue Click here to Get API Key for your Account Select your QuestionPro account and click on Save + Continue Select the survey from your QuestionPro account whose responses you want to gather for that zap and click on Continue Zapier will pull some pre-recorded responses (samples) from the survey selected in previous window. Check the response and click on Continue Choose any Action app for your zap where you want to send the responses Let's take an example of Google Sheets as an Action app. Select the action you want to perform on your Google sheet and click on Save + Continue Connect your Google account to access the sheets by clicking on Connect an Account and then click on Save + Continue Select the Spreadsheet and Worksheet, which will load the column headers present in your sheet. Now map the data which will be loaded from the response in the column headers and click on Continue Finally, enter the name for your zap and switch the toggle ON which will activate your zap Actions You can also use QuestionPro as an action app on Zapier which will perform the available actions on the data fetched from the trigger apps. Send Survey: With the help of this action you can fetch the email address of the respondent from any trigger app and deploy a survey to that email address from your QuestionPro account. Note: Zapier uses your QuestionPro account's API calls to collect data, hence API calls will be recorded under your account's usage.       "id": "webhook",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Webhook",
      "summary": "What is Webhook?",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/webhook.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:14:06.919Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "What is Webhook?"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "What is Webhook? A webhook in web development is a method of augmenting or altering the behavior of a web application, with custom callbacks. These callbacks may be maintained, modified, and managed by third-party users and developers who may not necessarily be affiliated with the originating application. QuestionPro supports both incoming and outgoing webhooks. Webhooks can be invoked before the survey starts, during the survey (before or after a question) or after the survey is completed. What are incoming webhooks? Incoming Webhooks are a simple way to fetch the data from external sources into your survey. What are outgoing webhooks? Outgoing webhooks can be used to send survey data to an external source. They make use of normal HTTP requests with a JSON payload. How to set up webhooks? To create a webhook: Go to: Survey >> Integrations >> Webhooks Click on the Add New Webhook button. Enter a description for your webhook Select the required type (incoming / outgoing) Choose when the webhook should be invoked. Click on Save Changes Once the webhook has been added, you’ll need to provide the data required to make the API call: API URL: The endpoint URL for making an API call. Method: The HTTP method used for making the call. Request Header: HTTP header fields which are components of the header section of the request Request JSON: Payload data required by the API. You can also use the Insert Response button to insert the response in the Request JSON. Once you fill in the above details, click on the Save button. How to use retrieved data from an incoming webhook? You can save the data retrieved from the external resource by mapping it to custom variables. To open up the mapping dialog box click on Add Custom Mapping buton: You can map the data to custom variables by assigning individual JSON keys to specific custom variables. You can also assign a relevant name to the custom variables: Once the mapping is set, the collected data can be displayed in the survey using the following code: ${DisplayName} How to provide data for an outgoing webhook? In case of outgoing webhooks you can send the data collected in custom variables to an external web service by adding the custom variables on the Request JSON: Data Replacement Variables To fetch the Custom Variables data you can use the following tags for the corresponding Custom Variables in the request JSON: Custom Variable 1: ${custom1} Custom Variable 2: ${custom2} Custom Variable 3: ${custom3} Custom Variable 4: ${custom4} Custom Variable 5: ${custom5} Custom Variable N: ${customN} How would I know whether the Webhook was executed successfully or failed? QuestionPro provides logs facility for each webhook where you can check the API details which was called by that webhook along with the payload and the response from the endpoint. To access the logs go to a particular webhook and scroll to the bottom of the page where you can view the requests made by that webhook and on clicking on the ID you can check the details of any specific call. Also, if some outgoing calls failed to push the data, a Manual Retry button will be available in front of that API calls on which you can click anytime to push the data back to the API endpoint.           "level": "H1",
          "text": "Integrating QuestionPro to MTurk"

      "steps": [],
      "content": "Integrating QuestionPro to MTurk What is MTurk? Amazon Mechanical Turk (MTurk) is a crowdsourcing marketplace that makes it easier for individuals and businesses to outsource their processes and jobs to a distributed workforce who can perform these tasks virtually. This could include anything from conducting simple data validation and research to more subjective tasks like survey participation, content moderation, and more. MTurk enables companies to harness the collective intelligence, skills, and insights from a global workforce to streamline business processes, augment data collection and analysis, and accelerate machine learning development. Alternatives to MTruck for your respondent panel.- QuestionPro Audience .https://www.questionpro.com/audience/ Steps to connect QuestionPro to MTurk Paste the survey link from QuestionPro on MTurk, you’ll want to start from the Survey Link template. This enables you to post a task (HIT) on MTurk that directs Workers to your survey. Step 2 You will be prompted to provide a name, description, and keywords for your survey, be as descriptive as possible and include an estimate of how long your survey will take. You can then specify how much you will pay for each response and the number of respondents (Workers) you want to participate in your survey. Step 3 Select Auto-Approval settings for your Project. This value tells MTurk how long to wait before automatically approving a Worker’s response. If you set this value too short, you may not leave yourself enough time to fully review Workers’ responses before they are automatically approved. If you set this value too long, Workers may become frustrated at the delay in receiving payment upon completion of the survey. Customers conducting surveys tend to find that three days is a good balance, but please adjust to suit your survey requirements. Tracking responses You can do this either by providing Workers with a unique completion code or collecting a Worker’s ID in the survey itself. A unique completion code when complete you can then direct Workers to provide it in the MTurk task when they are done. You can then link the survey response to the Worker in your results. Instructions for doing this with QuestionPro are below Assigning Random IDs to Respondents You may need to create a random ID for each of your respondents. This is useful if you are running a study with MTurk where you ask respondents to prove their completion with an ID. To generate a Random ID there are a few steps. First select Survey Options in the menu for your survey. Generating a Completion Code in QuestionPro To generate a unique completion code here are a few steps. Go to the question in your survey that asks Workers for their Worker ID or email address and click on logic: Step 2 Step 3 In order to Display the Embedded Data in a question click the question to edit it. Add custom code on the Thank you page, to show the unique code after completing the survey.       "id": "webhooks-v2",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Webhooks",
      "summary": "What is Webhook?",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/webhooks-v2.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:14:18.922Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Webhooks"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Webhooks What is a Webhook? A webhook in web development is a method of augmenting or altering the behavior of a web application with custom callbacks. These callbacks may be maintained and managed by third-party users and developers who may not necessarily be affiliated with the originating application. QuestionPro supports both incoming and outgoing webhooks. Webhooks can be invoked before the survey starts, during the survey (before or after a question), or after the survey is completed. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download the video What are incoming webhooks? Incoming Webhooks are a simple way to fetch the data from external sources into your survey. What are outgoing webhooks? Outgoing webhooks can be used to send survey data to an external source. They use standard HTTP requests with a JSON payload. How to set up webhooks? To create a webhook: Choose the survey you want to setup the webhook for. Go to: Integrations >> Webhooks Click on the Add New Webhook button. Enter a description for your webhook and click on the Create Webhook button. Once you are in the configuration page, choose when the webhook should be invoked by selecting the trigger event. A webhook can be invoked before the survey, after a question, after a page in the survey, after the save and continue button in the survey, and after the survey. Note: For the 'After Question' trigger event, the webhook triggers at the end of the survey, although sending only data captured up to that question and not the full response. If you require the webhook to be triggered immediately 'After question', you must manually add a page break after the question. If you choose to trigger the webhook after the survey, you can also configure the conditions based on which the webhook should be triggered. Configure the conditions (if any) and click Next. After choosing the trigger condition, provide the data required for the API call: Method: The HTTP method used for making the call. API URL: The endpoint URL for making an API call. Authorization type: Choose the authorization type and add credentials if required. You can also choose to add the credentials either to the header or parameters as required by the external source. Request Header: HTTP header fields which are components of the header section of the request. Request JSON: Payload data required by the API. You can also use the Insert Response button to insert the response in the Request JSON. Once you fill in the above details, click on the Test Run button to check if you are getting the desired output. If not, please retry after modifying the API request data. Once you are satisfied with the output, click Next. The Data Mapping step is primarily required for incoming webhooks. You can save the data retrieved from the external source by mapping it to variables. Please note that these variables are different from the custom variables that are stored with the respondent's response. If you don't want to map data to the variables you can proceed and click Save. You can map the data to variables by assigning individual JSON keys to specific variables. You can add and delete the variables as and when needed. Once the mapping is set, the collected data can be displayed in the survey using the following code: ${code}. Finally, once you want your webhook live, enable the toggle to make the webhook active. How to provide data for an outgoing webhook? In case of outgoing webhooks you can send the data collected in custom variables to an external web service by adding the custom variables on the Request JSON. Data Replacement Variables To fetch the Custom Variable data, you can use the following tags for the corresponding Custom Variables in the request JSON: Custom Variable 1: ${custom1} Custom Variable 2: ${custom2} Custom Variable 3: ${custom3} Custom Variable 4: ${custom4} Custom Variable 5: ${custom5} Custom Variable N: ${customN} How would I know whether the Webhook was executed successfully or failed? QuestionPro provides a logging facility for each webhook where you can check the API details called by that webhook along with the payload and the response from the endpoint. To access the logs, go to a particular webhook and click on Logs, where you can view the requests made by that webhook and on clicking on the ID you can check the details of any specific call. Also, if the outgoing call failed to push the data for an After Survey event, a Manual Retry button will be available next to any failed API calls, which you can click at any time to resend the data to the API endpoint. You can also select multiple webhooks and retry in bulk. Other trigger events are not supported for retry mechanism. Are the conditions supported on all trigger events? No. Currently conditions are only supported for After Survey event. What are the question types supported under conditions? We currently support Select one, Select many, Drop-down menu, and Single row text. How can I display the variable data within the survey? If you want to display the value of a variable like ${customN} in the survey, you can easily use ${code}. Alternatively you can add the same survey-level custom variable mapping that you configured in the data mapping section of the webhook or add a page break before that question. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.",

      "content": "Migration Center What is the Migration Center? The Migration Center is your one-stop solution for moving to QuestionPro — designed to save you time, eliminate hassle, and ensure a seamless transition from your current survey platform. Whether you're coming from SurveyMonkey, Qualtrics, or Decipher, we've built import tools that do the heavy lifting for you. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video Why businesses choose to migrate to QuestionPro? Save valuable time: What used to take days of manual work now happens in just a few hours. Zero data loss: Your surveys and responses transfer completely intact, preserving years of data. Effortless process: Our intuitive import tools guide you through migration in just a few clicks. Secure and reliable: Our Industry-standard import tools ensure your data stays protected throughout the transfer. Bulk migration: Import up to 50 surveys at once, which is perfect for teams managing large survey portfolios. The best part is that you don't need to be tech-savvy. Our Migration Center handles all the complexity behind the scenes, so you can focus on what matters most. How can I access the Migration Center? To access the Migration Center, please follow the steps below: Log in to your QuestionPro account. Navigate to the My Account dropdown and click on the Migration Center option. You'll see all available migration tools displayed on this screen. What are the available import tools? The Migration Center currently supports migration from the following platforms: SurveyMonkey Import Tool: Import your SurveyMonkey surveys and responses using the SurveyMonkey API. Click here to learn more about the SurveyMonkey Import Tool. Decipher Import Tool: Import your Decipher surveys and responses using the Decipher API. Click here to learn more about the Decipher Import Tool. Qualtrics Import Tool: Import your Qualtrics surveys and responses using one of two methods:API Method: Import using Qualtrics API. Click here to learn more about the Qualtrics Import Tool - API. QSF and CSV Method: Import using QSF and CSV files. Click here to learn more about the Qualtrics Import Tool - QSF. How does the migration process work? The migration process follows these general steps: Click on the desired migration tool tile from the Migration Center. Click on the Import Surveys button. Enter your API credentials for the source platform. For Qualtrics, you can also import surveys and responses using the QSF and CSV files. Our system will fetch all the surveys from your source platform and display the list in the pop-up. Select the surveys you want to import using the checkboxes. Configure import options (response import preferences, destination folder). Click the Import button to proceed. The import process will start and you'll be notified via email after the process is complete. For detailed step-by-step instructions specific to each platform, please refer to the individual help documentation linked above. How can I check the list of surveys imported? To check the list of surveys imported in a specific batch, click on the count of surveys displayed next to the batch. The pop-up will open displaying the list of the surveys along with the status of survey import and response import. You can click on the survey name to preview the survey or after hovering on the survey name, click on the Open in Workspace icon to open and edit the survey. How can I check the logs of the imported batch? To view the detailed logs for the process, click on the View Logs option after hovering over the import batch. The logs will appear in a pop-up. Here you can also select to view logs for a specific survey by selecting the survey from the dropdown menu. What can I import from my current survey platform to QuestionPro? Depending on the platform you're migrating from, the following can be imported: Survey questions and answer choices. Response data (completed responses, partial responses, or no responses based on your selection). Response metadata (timestamps, IP addresses, completion status, etc.). Survey logic (availability varies by platform - check individual import tool documentation.) For platform-specific details about supported question types and features, please refer to the individual import tool help files. FAQs How many surveys can I import at a time? You can import a maximum of 50 surveys at a time in a single batch. What API credentials do I need? The required credentials vary by platform: SurveyMonkey: Access token and Server domain. Qualtrics: API key/OAuth credentials and Datacenter ID. Decipher: API key and Server domain. Will I be notified when the import is complete? Yes. You'll receive an email notification at the email address associated with your QuestionPro account once the import process is complete. Note: Make sure you have the required API call limits on your source platform subscription before starting the migration process. This tool allows you to migrate all your surveys along with the collected responses from Qualtrics to QuestionPro using Qualtrics API. Your browser does not support the video tag. Click to download video How can I import Qualtrics surveys in QuestionPro? For migrating your surveys from Qualtrics to QuestionPro, please follow the steps below: Login to your QuestionPro account. Navigate to My Account dropdown and click on the Migration Center option Click on the Qualtrics Import Tool tile Click on the API tab On this screen, click on the Import Surveys button. On the pop-up, enter your Qualtrics API key and datacenter ID. Then click on the Next button. Our system will fetch all the surveys from Qualtrics and display the list in the pop-up. Select the surveys you want to import by using the checkboxes. The selected surveys will be displayed on the right section of the pop-up. After selecting a survey, you'll find a dropdown menu at the bottom-right of the pop-up which will allow you to enable or disable response import for all the selected surveys. You can also choose whether to import logic using a toggle. Additionally, you can select the destination folder for the surveys using the dropdown in the top-right corner of the pop-up. Once you've made your selections, click the Import button to proceed. The import process will be started and you'll be notified via email after the process completion. Note: Make sure you have the required API call limit on your Qualtrics subscription. How can I check the list of surveys imported? To check the list of surveys imported in a specific batch, click on the count of surveys displayed near the batch. The pop-up will open up displaying the list of the surveys along with the status of survey and responses import. You can click on the survey name to preview the survey or after hovering on the survey name, click on the Open in Workspace icon to open and edit the survey. How can I check the logs of the imported batch? To view the detailed logs for the process, click on the View Logs option after hovering over the import batch. The logs will appear in the pop-up. Here you can also select to view logs for a specific survey by selecting the survey from the dropdown menu. Can I use OAuth instead of API key/token to import the surveys? Yes, you can use OAuth instead of API key/token to import the surveys. To switch the authentication to OAuth, click on the dropdown for Select Authentication Type and choose OAuth as the authentication type. For OAuth, you'll need to provide client ID, client secret, and the datacenter ID. Then hit the next button and the rest of the process is the same as mentioned above. Can I import survey logics from Qualtrics? Yes, you can now import survey logics from Qualtrics by just turning ON the toggle at the bottom right, Import Logic during the import process. Following are the supported logics for this feature: Skip logic Custom Validation Display Logic - Questions (Show/Hide Question) Display Logic - Answers (Show/Hide Options) Scoring Logic Unsupported Question Types for Qualtrics Import Tool File Upload Highlight (Speciality Question) Drill Down (Speciality Question) Timing (Advanced) Meta Info Question (Advanced) Screen Capture (Advanced) Side-by-Side Matrix HeatMap Hotspot Card Sorting Matrix Table: Dropdown list Conjoint and MaxDiff FAQs How many surveys I can import at a time? The survey will be imported in your QuestionPro account but the Compound branch logic will not be applied on the survey. QuestionPro does not import Compund Branch logic present in the Qualtrics survey. However you can apply the logic on your survey after importing it. While importing the survey responses via this integration, does QuestionPro import custom fields as well? The basic response metadata fields are startDate, endDate, ipAddress, finished, recipientEmail, userLanguage, duration, locationLatitude, and locationLongitude. Click to download video How can I import Decipher surveys into QuestionPro? To migrate your surveys from Decipher to QuestionPro, please follow the steps below: Log in to your QuestionPro account. Navigate to the My Account dropdown and click on the Migration Center option. Click on the Decipher Import Tool tile. Click on the Import surveys button. In the pop-up, enter your Decipher API key and Server domain (e.g., emea.focusvision.com). Then click on the Next button. Our system will fetch all the surveys from Decipher and display the list in the pop-up. Select the surveys you want to import by using the checkboxes. The selected surveys will be displayed on the right side of the pop-up. After selecting surveys, you'll find a dropdown menu at the bottom right of the pop-up which will allow you to import all responses, completed responses, or no responses for all the selected surveys. Additionally, you can select the destination folder for the surveys using the dropdown in the top right corner of the pop-up. Once you've made your selections, click the Import button to proceed. The import process will start and you'll be notified via email after the process is complete. Note: Make sure you have the required API call limits on your Decipher subscription. How can I check the list of surveys imported? To check the list of surveys imported in a specific batch, click on the count of surveys displayed next to the batch. The pop-up will open displaying the list of surveys along with the status of survey import and response import. You can click on the survey name to preview the survey or after hovering on the survey name, click on the Open in Workspace icon to open and edit the survey. How can I check the logs of the imported batch? To view the detailed logs for the process, click on the View Logs option after hovering over the import batch. The logs will appear in a pop-up. Here you can also select to view logs for a specific survey by selecting the survey from the dropdown menu.       "title": "Connect QuestionPro MCP servers to ChatGPT",
      "summary": "Learn how to connect QuestionPro MCP servers to ChatGPT using Apps. Add any QuestionPro MCP server to ChatGPT in a few steps using OAuth — no API keys required.",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/connect-questionpro-mcp-chatgpt.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:15:04.916Z",

          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Connect QuestionPro MCP Servers to ChatGPT"
        }
