      "id": "can-spam-compliance-tool",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "CAN-SPAM Compliance",
      "summary": "CAN-SPAM Compliance ",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/can-spam-compliance-tool.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:19:22.927Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "CAN-SPAM Compliance"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "CAN-SPAM Compliance The CAN-SPAM Act of 2003 (Controlling the Assault of Non-Solicited Pornography and Marketing Act) establishes requirements for those who send commercial email, spells out penalties for spammers and companies whose products are advertised in spam if they violate the law, and gives consumers the right to ask emailers to stop spamming them. The law, which became effective January 1, 2004, covers email whose primary purpose is advertising or promoting a commercial product or service, including content on a Web site. A \"transactional or relationship message\", an email that facilitates an agreed-upon transaction or updates a customer in an existing business relationship, may not contain false or misleading routing information, but otherwise is exempt from most provisions of the CAN-SPAM Act. The Federal Trade Commission (FTC), the nation's consumer protection agency, is authorized to enforce the CAN-SPAM Act. CAN-SPAM also gives the Department of Justice (DOJ) the authority to enforce its criminal sanctions. Other federal and state agencies can enforce the law against organizations under their jurisdiction, and companies that provide Internet access may sue violators, as well. What does the law require? It bans false or misleading header information. Your email's \"From,\" \"To,\" and routing information, inncluding the originating domain name and email address, must be accurate and identify the person who initiated the email. It prohibits deceptive subject lines. The subject line cannot mislead the recipient about the contents or subject matter of the message. It requires that your email give recipients an opt-out method. You must provide a return email address or another Internet-based response mechanism that allows a recipient to ask you not to send future email messages to that email address, and you must honor the requests. You may create a \"menu\" of choices to allow a recipient to opt out of certain types of messages, but you must include the option to end any commercial messages from the sender. It requires that commercial email be identified as an advertisement and include the sender's valid physical postal address. Your message must contain clear and conspicuous notice that the message is an advertisement or solicitation and that the recipient can opt out of receiving more commercial email from you. It also must include your valid physical postal address. What about the first two requirements? Valid \"From\" email addresses and deceptive subject lines? Both these requirements are for you to fulfill. When sending out survey invitations please make sure that the subject lines are relevant and you always use your own email address as the \"From\" address. How do I comply with CAN-SPAM? Log in to your account. If CAN SPAM update is pending, you will see a banner for pending verifications. Click on the Verify link for CAN SPAM Fill in the correct details and click on the Update button to save. How do I update or change CAN-SPAM details? Go to: Surveys » Distribute » Email Under the Compose section, near the bottom right corner, click on the CAN-SPAM link to update details. Is CAN-SPAM Compliance a requirement? Is it really necessary? As of April 15th 2006, we have made it mandatory that all emails sent using QuestionPro comply with the CAN-SPAM law. If CAN-SPAM Compliance is not enabled, you will not be able to send emails using QuestionPro. I am sending survey invitations to my customers/clients. Do I need to have the CAN-SPAM Compliance? Yes - All emails (including survey invitations) sent using QuestionPro must be CAN-SPAM compliant. The services for QuestionPro operate out of the United States and we need to follow federal US laws. This cannot be turned off. I don't live in the USA. Is this still necessary? Yes. Irrespective of the country you live in, we are legally required to include a physical address of the sender in every email. You will need to comply with CAN-SPAM address verification. CAN-SPAM verification will help with improving email delivery and benefits email delivery to the inbox directly. License This feature is available with the following licenses : Essentials Advanced Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.",

      "id": "respondent-anonymity-assurance-raa",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Respondent anonymity assurance",
      "summary": "Respondent Anonymity Assurance (RAA)",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/respondent-anonymity-assurance-raa.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:19:41.928Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Respondent Anonymity Assurance (RAA)"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [
        "The ability to track respondents response status so reminders or follow up emails can be sent,",
        "For human subjects protocols or for other privacy reasons, ensuring that email identifications not be linked to the response data."
      ],
      "content": "Respondent Anonymity Assurance (RAA) One of the challenges researchers face is the requirement for two directly conflicting issues The ability to track respondents response status so reminders or follow up emails can be sent, AND For human subjects protocols or for other privacy reasons, ensuring that email identifications not be linked to the response data. From a technical standpoint, if we need to track who has taken the survey and who has not, the survey researcher also implicitly has the ability to track the response for each individual. To overcome this issue, the Respondent Anonymity Assurance has been introduced. QuestionPro asserts that once RAA is enabled on a survey, although computer generated identification numbers for individuals will be generated, the survey researcher will not have access to both the respondent's email address as well as the response data at the same time. for more information please visit: Respondent Anonymity Assurance The Survey should be distributed using QuestionPro Email Management and the Survey Tracking link should be used for distribution. What assertions does QuestionPro make for RAA-enabled surveys? Once the RAA is enabled for a survey, at the request of the survey researcher, it will remain perpetual and cannot be rescinded -- by the researcher or anyone else. The QuestionPro Survey Software will never present a respondent's email address linked to the response data in any of the analysis tools, reports and data downloads. In the exceptionally unlikely event that a breach occurs for any reason, technical or otherwise, QuestionPro will immediately notify the respondent as well as the survey researcher about the issue. If the respondent requests his response data to be removed from the servers as a result of the breach, QuestionPro will comply with the request even if the survey researcher does not agree. Furthermore, QuestionPro will make all reasonable efforts to make sure that such a breach does not occur again. How do I enable RAA for my survey? Go to: Login » Surveys (Select a survey) » Edit » Settings Enable the toggle switch for Respondents Anonymity Assurance On the popup, confirm that you wish to enable RAA. Once enabled, it is not possible to disable RAA. I have tracking information passed as custom variables. Can I hide it? (SSO authenticated survey) Yes. You can hide information stored in custom variables 1-5. To do so, enable the option Mask Custom Variables 1-5 This will hide/block information stored in custom variables 1 to 5. You will still be able to use logic as well as filtering in aggregate. How do I know if a survey I am taking is protected by RAA? For surveys that have RAA enabled, a link to the details about the guarantee will be put on the footer of the survey. This link cannot be removed or added by the survey researcher. It will be automatically added on surveys that have the anonymity guarantee enabled. Once this link is enabled it cannot be removed, changed or edited. How do the Reports look? Any and all data which can be used for respondent identification will be masked using the text: \"**RAA Blocked**\". Which fields get hidden when RAA is turned on? Following are the fields which get hidden when RAA is turned on: Respondent Email IP Address Country Code Region License This feature is available with the following licenses : Team Edition Research Edition Was the article helpful?       "content": "Security Introduction This document outlines the updated security measures and protocols in place at QuestionPro. Our commitment to safeguarding data and ensuring the integrity of our systems is paramount. This document includes recent enhancements to our security infrastructure, including the integration of Cloudflare's Web Application Firewall (WAF), advanced network firewall strategies, and the utilization of VPNs for secure remote access. QuestionPro - Cloudflare WAF Integration Cloudflare's WAF is a cutting-edge, cloud-based solution that provides robust protection against a variety of cyber threats targeting web applications, APIs, and websites. Advantages: Enhanced Security: Offers robust defense against common vulnerabilities like SQL injection and XSS attacks. DDoS Protection: Effectively mitigates the risks of DDoS attacks. Performance Optimization: Improves website performance through global caching and optimization strategies. Seamless Integration: Easily integrates with existing infrastructures, eliminating the need for physical hardware. Network Firewalls Overview: Network centralized hardware firewalls serve as a primary defense mechanism against external threats, monitoring and controlling the flow of data in and out of the network based on pre-established security rules. In the evolving digital landscape, securing systems against external threats is crucial. Traditional firewalls have been effective since the 1980s, but Next-Generation Firewalls (NGFWs) blend traditional and modern technologies for optimal digital protection. Top 5 Firewall Benefits: Monitors Network Traffic: Effectively analyzes incoming and outgoing data to prevent threats. Empowers IT teams to manage protection levels based on observed network activity. Stops Virus Attacks: Controls system entry points to thwart virus attacks. Mitigates potential immeasurable damage caused by rapidly evolving threats. Prevents Hacking: Crucial defense against unauthorized access to data, emails, and systems. Deters hackers or stops them entirely, safeguarding against data theft. Stops Spyware: Blocks spyware and malware infiltration into complex systems. Acts as a vital blockade against unauthorized access and data theft. Promotes Privacy: Proactively protects data, fostering trust with customers. Enhances competitiveness by showcasing robust data protection as a selling point. Network Firewall Advantages for Enhanced Security: Our network security with Next-Generation Firewalls (NGFWs) with numerous benefits: Versatility: Real-time upgrades provide flexibility and adaptability. Centralized deployment facilitates quick adjustments to changing threat landscapes. Intelligent Port Control: Multi-layered protection surpasses traditional single-layer port approaches. Application-level security ensures better control, transparency, and data management. Simple Infrastructure: Streamlined infrastructure allows easy deployment of policies from one device. Transparent controls grant flexibility in managing network access. Updated Threat Protection: Integrated security measures (firewall, antivirus, ransomware protection, intrusion detection) enhance cooperation and simplify management. Deep packet inspection (DPI) provides a more informed decision-making process for data flow. Consistent Network Speed: NGFWs maintain high throughput without compromising security. Protect systems without sacrificing operational speed. VPN (Virtual Private Network) Usage Description: VPNs extend a private network across a public network, allowing secure data transmission as if the devices were directly connected to the private network. Key features: Secure Remote Access: Ensures secure access to corporate resources from any location. Data Encryption: Provides robust encryption for secure data communication. Privacy and Anonymity: Maintains user privacy and anonymity online. Geographical Freedom: Enables access to regionally restricted content and services. Understanding the advantages and potential drawbacks of Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) is crucial for informed decision-making within your organization. Despite some limitations, the benefits of using a VPN can significantly enhance your business's security profile. Pros of VPN: Secure Your Network: Effectively safeguards network integrity by preventing unauthorized access. Protects transmitted data, ensuring security and anonymity. Hide Your Private Information: High-level security, such as 256-bit encryption, conceals sensitive information. Mitigates the risk of hackers intercepting and misusing personal data. Prevent Data Throttling: Avoids service slowdowns imposed by internet service providers (ISPs) after data consumption. Conceals data usage, especially beneficial for mobile use and remote work. Avoid Bandwidth Throttling: Encrypts mobile traffic, preventing ISPs from intentionally slowing down internet speed. Enhances privacy by masking visited websites, reducing the likelihood of throttling. Get Access to Geo-blocked Services: Acquire a different IP address, enabling access to restricted content based on location. Facilitates unrestricted use of online services and information. Network Scalability: Cost-effective solution for expanding network access to multiple employees and remote workers. Enables running key applications in a secure cloud environment through the VPN tunnel. Reduce Support Costs: Shifts maintenance, performance checks, and security responsibilities to the VPN service provider. Offers potential cost savings by leveraging the service provider's infrastructure management expertise. Site-to-Site VPN Overview: A site-to-site Virtual Private Network (VPN) establishes a secure and encrypted connection between VPN gateways situated at different locations. This VPN tunnel encrypts data at its source, sends it over the public Internet, and decrypts it at the destination site before routing it to its final destination. Benefits of Site-to-Site VPN: Secure Connectivity: Encrypts all traffic passing through the site-to-site VPN, ensuring the protection of business data against eavesdropping and unauthorized modification during transmission over the public Internet. Simplified Network Architecture: Facilitates the use of internal IP address ranges for devices within LANs. Eliminates the need to convert internal addresses to external ones for public Internet accessibility, maintaining the \"internal\" status of traffic between LANs. Access Control: Streamlines access control by treating site-to-site VPN users as \"internal\" users. Simplifies rule definition, allowing for easier management of network resources, particularly those restricted to internal access only. External users are inherently blocked from accessing these resources.       "content": "California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Compliance What is CCPA Compliance? Link Copied The California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) is a bill intended to enhance privacy rights and consumer protection for residents of California, United States. The intentions of the Act are to provide California residents with the right to: Know what personal data is being collected about them. Know whether their personal data is sold or disclosed and to whom. Say no to the sale of personal data. Access their personal data. Request a business to delete any personal information about a consumer collected from that consumer. Not be discriminated against for exercising their privacy rights.           "text": "QuestionPro Accessibility Statement"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "QuestionPro Accessibility Statement Survey Respondent Interface Accessible Layout At QuestionPro, we are committed to making our surveys accessible to everyone, including users with visual, hearing, motor, or cognitive impairments. Keyboard Navigation: On desktop devices, surveys can be completed without a mouse. Use the Tab key to navigate between elements and the Enter key to make selections. Screen Reader Support: Our survey platform is compatible with popular screen readers. We recommend using JAWS version 16 or later, as well as the latest version of Google Chrome for best results. Standards Compliance: The respondent experience in QuestionPro surveys is designed to comply with: Section 508 of the U.S. Rehabilitation Act European Accessibility Act (EAA) WCAG 2.1 Level AA EN 301 549 (European accessibility requirements for ICT products and services) If you encounter accessibility issues or need assistance, please contact your Account Manager or email us at support-team@questionpro.com. How to enable accessibility on a survey? Go to: Surveys (Select Survey) » Edit Click on on the Design tab. Under Survey Layout, select the Accessible layout. Save and Apply and your survey will be accessibility compliant. Which are the question types compatible and supported for accessibility? For accessibility, we want to make the surveys as easy as possible to answer. Keeping the surveys simple should be the primary goal. To achive this, certain question types like drag and drop, image hotspot or heatmap, and complex questions like flex matrix, etc. aren't supported. Settings like auto-advance and auto-submit along with tools like exit survey button are disabled on 508 compliant or accessible surveys to ease navigation through the survey. Following is a complete list of question types with accessibilitiy support: Basic Question Types: Multiple Choice Select One Select Many Drop-down Menu Text Comment Box Single Row Text Email Address Contact Information Graphical Rating Star Rating Smiley - Rating Thumbs Up/Down Image Chooser Select One Select Many Rating Ordering Rank Order Constant Sum Basic Matrix Multi-Point Scales Multi-Select Spreadsheet Static Content Presentation Text Section Heading Section Sub-Heading Misc Date / Time Calendar Advanced Question Types Customer Satisfaction Net Promoter Score Pricing Analysis Van Westendorp Data Reference Reference Data Lookup Table Logic Custom Logic Engine Survey administration and editing interface The QuestionPro administrative and editing interface has not been certified or tested for accessibility. QuestionPro will strive to provide support for, and facilitate the accommodation of individuals with disabilities so that all may share the same level of access to the Product/Surveys.       "content": "QuestionPro Data Centers What is a data center? A data center is a building, a dedicated space within a building, or a group of buildings used to house computer systems and associated components, such as telecommunications and storage systems. Where does QuestionPro have its data centers? We have the following data centers for different regions: US Seattle, USA Dallas, USA Canada: Toronto, CA Europe (EU): Meppel, Netherlands Middle East and North Africa (MENA): Dubai, UAE Kingdom of Saudi Arabia: Riyadh, KSA Australia: Victoria, AU India: Mumbai, IN Can I create my account on any data center? Yes. You can create an account on any of our data centers. If you already have an account and wish to create an account on a different data center, please contact your account manager. To check your data center: Go to: My Account Click on the data center information. Can I move my data or surveys from one data center to another? No. Moving surveys or data between data centers is not possible. To comply with data security requirements on a global level as well as to comply with local regional legal requirements, we cannot and don't allow data to be moved between data centers.       "content": "Automated deletion of data As data protection and privacy become increasingly critical, organizations must adopt a proactive approach to minimizing the volume of stored data. Retaining only what is necessary not only reduces risk but also supports regulatory compliance. Automated data deletion solutions empower organizations to systematically and securely dispose of data in accordance with predefined policies and compliance requirements. This ensures that sensitive information is not held longer than needed, streamlining data governance while reinforcing customer trust and organizational accountability. Which license level is needed? Systematic data deletion is inherently complex, given the distributed architecture across multiple data centers and servers managed by QuestionPro. A client’s data may reside on one or more servers within a single data center, adding to the intricacy of the process. As a result, the capability to enable automated data deletion is available exclusively under an enterprise-wide license*. Entperprise-wide license means a organization with atleast two product verticals. (Example CX and Research) What types of data are eligible for automated deletion? All responses Incomplete responses How do I enable it? You need to contact your customer success manager, who will raise a ticket for the same. The team will need 2-6 weeks to setup the scheduled deletion process depending on the volume, complexity and product. Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it?     {
      "id": "onelogin-survey-sso",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "OneLogin- Survey Authentication SAML SSO",
      "summary": "OneLogin- Survey Authentication SAML SSO",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/onelogin-survey-sso.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:20:39.929Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "OneLogin- Survey Authentication SAML SSO"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "OneLogin- Survey Authentication SAML SSO Survey authentication SAML SSO is SP initiated single sign on, which will redirect the respondent to the IDP's login page before answering the survey. If the respondent is authenticated by IDP then and only then he/ she is able to answer the survey. How to set up Survey Authentication SSO using OneLogin as IDP? While setting up the SSO login authentication using OneLogin as IDP you have to upload the OneLogin metadata URL which contains a Single Sign On URL into you QuestionPro account under User Profile>>My Account>>SSO Authentication>>SAML (Signed). As you click Save Changes button the SSO URL is fetched by the tool and populated in the \"Single Sign On URL\" input field. This URL is used to redirect the respondent to the IDP for authentication when he/ she tries to answer the survey. After the above set up you'll get a new survey authentication option under Survey >> Settings >> Security named as \"(Organization Name) SAML\". Select this option and click on Save Changes. I have already done the set up for SSO login authentication SAML using OneLogin as IDP, how can I use it for survey authentication? For the existing SAML SSO login authentication set up, you'll need to copy the SSO URL from your OneLogin app and paste it in the Single Sign On URL input field and click on Save Changes. To get the SSO URL from OneLogin follow the steps below: Go to the existing application in your OneLogin account then click on Sign On tab. Here you can see SAML 2.0 Endpoint (HTTP) URl, copy this URL and paste it under \"SIngle Sign On URL\" and click on Save Changes. How do I pass additional user data from OneLogin to QuestionPro survey response? You can pass the additional user data from OneLogin to QuestionPro survey response by passing them as attributes in the XML Assertion. While setting up the application in OneLogin you can define the attributes under Parameters tab. Here you can provide any name to the attribute and select the value from the drop-down menu.       "id": "adfs-sso-setup",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "ADFS- SSO setup with QuestionPro",
      "summary": "Active Directory Federation Services (ADFS) sso setup with QuestionPro",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/adfs-sso-setup.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:20:51.928Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "ADFS- SSO setup with QuestionPro"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "ADFS- SSO setup with QuestionPro What is ADFS? Active Directory Federation Service (ADFS) is a Microsoft software component that provides Single Sign-On (SSO) authorization to users of Windows Server Operating Systems. ADFS enables users to access applications on Windows Server Operating Systems across organizational boundaries using a single set of login credentials. ADFS enables users from one organization to access applications from partner organizations by using their standard Active Directory credentials (AD). ADFS also allows users to access AD-integrated applications while working remotely via a web interface, using their standard organizational AD credentials. How do I connect QuestionPro and ADFS using SSO? Link Copied To connect ADFS and QuestionPro using SAML 2.0, follow the steps below: Install ADFS Go to: Start » All Programs » Administrative Tools, click on ADFS Management In the ADFS Management window, right click on Relying Party Trusts then click on Add Relying Party Trust In the setup wizard, select Claims aware and click on the Start button On the second step, select the Enter data about the replying party manually option and click on the Next button. On the third step, enter the name of the relying party trust and click on the Next button. On the fourth step, Upload the QuestionPro SP certificate and click on the Next button. Check out how to get the QuestionPro SP certificate here. On the fifth step, select Enable support for the SAML 2.0 SSO service URL, enter the ACS URL from your QuestionPro account, and click on the Next button. Check out how to get the ACS URL from your QuestionPro account here. On the sixth step, enter the Relying party trust identifier and click on the Add button on the right. Indentifier URL: https://www.questionpro.com/saml2 You'll see the identifier added in the Relying party trust identifier section. Then click on the Next button. On the seventh step, review the permission of this app for your organization and click on the Next button. On the eighth step, review all the details added for the relying party trust and click on the Next button. Finally on the ninth step, click on the Close button. Now a new window will open-up to add the Claim Issuance Policy. In this new window, click on the Add Rule button to add a new claim policy rule for QuestionPro In the pop-up, under Claim rule template select Send LDAP Attributes as Claims and click on the Next button. Enter the claim rule name as emailAddress, select E-Mail-Address as the LDAP Attribute, and enter emailAddress as the Outgoing Claim Type. Click on the Finish button. You'll see the new claim rule added under claim policy for QuestionPro. Click the Apply button and then the OK button to finish the setup. All the steps on ADFS are complete, now go to your QuestionPro account » User Profile » Global Settings » SSO Authentication and upload the following information: Issuer/Entity ID: The issuer or entity ID will be in this format https://ADFS_SERVER/adfs/services/trust replacing ADFS_SERVER with your ADFS domain Signing Certificate: Check out how to extract the signing certificate from ADFS here. Now, to test out the SSO connection go to https://ADFS_SERVER/adfs/ls/IDpInitiatedSignOn.aspx replacing ADFS_SERVER with your ADFS server name. Select the Sign in to one of the following sites: option, choose the QuestionPro app and click the Sign in button. It will prompt you to enter the AD credentials and after successful authentication, you'll be redirected to your QuestionPro account. Note: SP initiated SSO is not supported with ADFS and QuestionPro SSO connection. How to get the SP certificate and ACS URL from your QuestionPro account? Link Copied Follow the steps below to get the SP certificate and ACS URL from your QuestionPro account: To get the SP certificate and ACS URL, login to your QuestionPro account>> click on the user profile icon>> click on the Global Settings . Under Global Settings, click on the SSO Authentication in the left navigation menu. Select SAML (Signed) option from the SSO Authentication dropdown menu. Here, you'll find the ACS URL and the option to download the SP certificate. Note: These settings are visible to the primary user of your organization by default and the access can be provided to other users by using User roles and permissions. How to extract the signing certificate from ADFS? Link Copied Follow the steps below to extract the signing certificate from ADFS: Go to: ADFS Management » Service » Certificates. Double click on Token-signing certificate or right-click on Token-signing certificate and then click on View Certificate. In the pop-up window, go to the Details tab and click on the Copy to File button to export the certificate. The Certificate Export Wizard will open up click on the Next button to start the process. On the second step, choose Base-64 encoded X.509 (.CER) option and click on the Next button. On the third step, select the path where you want to export the certificate, enter the name for the file and click on the Next button. On the final step, click on the Finish button Can we map new users to a specific Business Unit/ Team? Link Copied Yes, It is possible to map new users to a specific Business Unit/ Team. You can pass a claim named \"teamName\" in your SAML assertion containing the Business Unit Name / Team Name from your QuestionPro account which will add new users under that specific Business Unit/ Team. In the following example shown in the screenshot we have used the \"Department\" field of the user's profile to store the Team Name for the user which will be passed in the SAML assertion: License This feature is available with the following license : Research Edition Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? Submit Thank you for submitting feedback.",

      "id": "azure-sso-setup",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Microsoft Azure- SSO set up with QuestionPro",
      "summary": "Microsoft Azure - SSO setup with QuestionPro",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/azure-sso-setup.html",
      "scraped_at": "2026-06-17T11:20:57.924Z",
      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Microsoft Azure - SSO setup with QuestionPro"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [
        "Microsoft Entra Identifier → Issuer / Entity ID in QuestionPro.",
        "Login URL → Single Sign-On URL in QuestionPro.",
        "Logout URL → Logout URL in QuestionPro.",
        "Upload the certificate you downloaded from Azure by clicking the Choose file button."
      ],
      "content": "Microsoft Azure - SSO setup with QuestionPro What is Microsoft Azure? Microsoft Azure (formerly Windows Azure) is a leading cloud platform that provides a wide range of services for building, deploying, and managing applications through Microsoft-managed data centers. It includes tools for computing, data storage, networking, and identity management - all designed to help organizations securely run their operations in the cloud [Wiki]. What is Microsoft Entra ID? Microsoft Entra ID (previously known as Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management solution that enables organizations to manage user identities and control access to applications and resources securely [Wiki]. When setting up SSO with QuestionPro, Microsoft Entra ID acts as the Identity Provider (IdP), verifying user credentials and granting secure access without requiring multiple logins. This ensures a smooth, secure, and consistent login experience for all users within your organization. How to set up SSO with QuestionPro using Microsoft Azure (Microsoft Entra ID)? Follow the steps below to connect Microsoft Azure (Microsoft Entra ID) with QuestionPro: Log in to your Azure portal. Create a new Enterprise application under Microsoft Entra ID. Open the app and click Set up single sign on. Select the single sign-on method as SAML. Now click on the pencil icon to edit the Basic SAML Configuration. Under Basic SAML Configuration, add https://www.questionpro.com/saml2 under Identifier (Entity ID) field and paste the ACS URL from your QuestionPro account into the Reply URL (Assertion Consumer Service URL) field. Click Save. Click the pencil icon to edit Attributes and Claims. Click Add new claim. In the next page that opens, enter the Name of the attribute as emailAddress and select the Source attribute as user.mail. Click Save. Scroll to the bottom of the Single sign-on page. Under SAML Certificates, click the Download link next to Certificate (Base64). Then, copy the Login URL, Microsoft Entra Identifier, and Logout URL. Go to your QuestionPro account and navigate to User Profile >> My Account. Under SSO Authentication, select SAML (Signed) from the drop-down menu and choose Manual Settings as the Configuration Type. Paste the values from your Azure app as follows: Microsoft Entra Identifier → Issuer / Entity ID in QuestionPro. Login URL → Single Sign-On URL in QuestionPro. Logout URL → Logout URL in QuestionPro. Upload the certificate you downloaded from Azure by clicking the Choose file button. Click Save. To test your SSO connection, go to the Single sign-on page in your Azure application and scroll to the bottom. Click the Test button, then in the right-side pop-up, click Test sign in. You will be logged directly into your QuestionPro account. Note: If you do not have an existing QuestionPro account with the email address used in your Microsoft Azure (Entra ID) platform, a sub-account will be created under your organization (due to Just-In-Time provisioning), and you will be logged into that sub-account. How to assign my Azure application to a user or a group of users? To assign the application to users, follow the steps below: Open your Azure application and click Users and groups under “Manage” in the left navigation. Then click Add user/group. Click the Users section. On the right-side pop-up search for and select the user or group to whom you want to assign the application, then click Select at the bottom. Widget Insights: Widget insights is a feature that will generate insights automatically for the widgets added to the dashboard. These insights are based on the various data points, question text, answer segments, and other elements present within the widget. The “Comment” option will be shown when you hover over any insight; using it you can comment on specific insight. Notes: Owner of the dashboard can delete any insight and comment in the insights sectio Users can edit or delete their own insights or comments using the Edit and Delete options provided on the insights and comments.       "id": "DataSlicers",
      "product": "Surveys",
      "title": "Data Slicers",
      "summary": "Data Slicers",
      "url": "https://www.questionpro.com/help/DataSlicers.html",

      "headings": [
        {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Data Slicers"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Data Slicers What is a data slice? Data Slice is a set of respondents out of the total respondents that can be identified based on a single or multiple criteria. It basically represents a subpopulation out of the total population. What is the data slicers functionality? Data slicer functionality enables the dashboard users to create data slices of their own choice for analysis purposes. Using this functionality multiple data slices can be created and added to the dashboard which can help to do comparative analysis based on certain criteria or variables against the total responses. Data slices can be created and added to a dashboard to identify the level of performance among the different divisions of a company. Further, it can be used to highlight and identify the areas of concern and set the investment priorities accordingly. Data slicers provide the ability to do comparative analysis and derive valuable insights from vast data sets. It also helps to enhance communication with stakeholders and improve engagement with the target audience. How to create and apply a data slicer to a dashboard? Creating data slices: Once you are on the dashboard click on the “Dashboard Settings” icon on the top right corner of the dashboard. In the “Dashboard Settings” popup inside the Data Slicers pane, click on the “Create Data Slicer” button to create a data slice. On the new pop-up to create a data slice, enter the name of the data slice along with criteria based on which you want to create the data slice. Criteria for Date range and survey response status can also be added based on requirements. Apply data slicers: A list of all the created data slices will be shown in the data slicer pane. From the available list based on your requirement, you can enable or disable the data slices from the provided chequebox under the “Apply to dashboard” option. All the selected data slices will be added to the dashboard. Interacting with data slicers: For Bar charts and Line charts dedicated bars and lines representing the enabled data slices on the respective dashboard will be added. It can be changed from the “Data Slicer View” option provide under the Anaytics section of the widget settings. Data Slicer Views: For few of the charts such as: Bar Chart and Line Chart, “Data Slicer View” option is provided, using which you can switch between two differnt views. 1. Combined - In combined view, all the bars and lines for the enabled data slices in the chart will be displayed combinely. 2. Seperate Tabs - In seperate tabs view, option to switch between all the enables data slices is provided in the chart and separate visuals are shown for each of the selection. Further, for all other types of charts, interactive buttons (same like Seperate Tabs view) will be added within the charts, using which the dashboard users can switch between different data slices. How to edit or delete a data slice? On the “Data Slicers” window in the “Dashboard Settings” you can edit or delete a data slice of your choice by clicking on the provided action buttons.       "steps": [],
      "content": "Create Dashboard How to create a dashboard? A dashboard allows you to add multiple visuals such as charts, tables, metrics, and others. These visuals are updated in real-time and assist its users to acquaint themselves with the most updated data and trends. These dashboards assist decision-makers in making vital decisions by providing important information about various factors and parameters at a glance. How do I create a dashboard? To create a dashboard from scratch, follow these steps: 1. Once you are logged in to your XA3 account, for the first time when you don't have any dashboards in your account; the system will show you a welcome message along with a “+ New Dashboard” button that can be used to create your first dashboard. 2. Once you click it, the system will ask for the name of the dashboard. If there are already a few dashboards present in your account and you want to create a new one, you can click on the “+New Dashboard” button on the left side. With this, your dashboard will be created in which you can start adding widgets of your choice. Quick actions: Renaming the dashboard, creating a copy of the existing dashboard, and deleting the dashboard are the quick actions you can do on a dashboard using the quick action buttons provided. Search dashboard: Using the search window provided on the dashboards page you can search for required dashboard Sort dashboards: You can sort the list of dashboards based on their names or date of creation. This can be done by clicking on the sort buttons provided in front of the “Dashboards” and “Created On” titles. Was the article helpful? Yes No How can we improve it? QuestionPro XA3 supports numerous widgets you can add as per your requirements using the “Add Widget” wizard. How do I add a widget to the dashboard To add widgets to your dashboard: Go to: The dashboard on which you want to add widgets Click on the “+ Add Widget” button or you can use the “+” button provided only for the first widget You can choose from the two categories of widgets mentioned as follows: Question Based - These are widgets based on a single survey question Advanced Widget - These are the widgets that take inputs from multiple parameters. It can be based on multiple questions, surveys, and datasets. Multiple other widgets that provide details about survey responses (such as Response Timeline, Text box, and Map) are also available under Advanced Widgets. Choose the appropriate folder and survey from the list Once you select the survey; next you can choose the question from the survey based on which you want to plot the widget. The steeper provided at the bottom will help you know the completed steps or the step which you are on to complete the widget addition process. The system will automatically show all the supported widgets for the question which you choose in the last step. Choose the widget of your choice. Widget Name: The system will automatically take the question text from the survey as widget name. You can enter the widget name of your choice in the provided “Widget Name” text box. This is a mandatory field. Description: This is an optional field. You can add any details related to the widget in the provided space. Click on the “Add Widget” button to add the widget to the dashboard. As shown below, the widget based on the details selected/added will be added to the dashboard. Resizing the widget - You can resize the widget by using the resize button provided at the right bottom corner of the widget. Moving or reordering the widget - to move the widget on the dashboard you can click and select the widget and move it over the dashboard and place it at the desired location. How do I delete widgets from a dashboard? Deleting a widget: Click on the widget settings icon of the widget you want to delete Click on the “Delete” icon from the menu A pop-up will appear to confirm whether you want to proceed further to delete the widget. Click on “Yes” to delete the widget permanently. Note: Delete action permanently deletes the widget and its associated data. Please double-check before deleting widgets. Was the article helpful?         {
          "level": "H1",
          "text": "Map Chart Widget"
        }
      ],
      "steps": [],
      "content": "Map Chart Widget What is a map chart widget A map chart widget can be used to plot the country-wise responses’ distribution in terms of count or percentages. This widget uses the country information from the metadata collected by the system to plot the responses. How do I add a map chart widget Once you are on the dashboard where you want to add the map chart widget >> Click on the “+Add Widget” button >> Click on “Advanced Widgets” >> select “Map Chart” and enter the widget name for the map chart widget >> Click on the Next button >> Select the survey for which you want to add the map chart widget >> Click on the “Save Widget” button Map Chart: Map Chart Settings: You can access the map chart settings by clicking on the widget settings icon on the top right corner of the widget and choosing “Settings” from it. General Settings: Under general settings, you can turn on or off the widget name and you can edit the widget name from here as well. You can turn on or off the highlighted insight for the widget under the general settings. Analytics Settings: Chart Metric - You can change the metric to be shown on the chart, either count of responses for countries or the percentage share of responses for countries. If you choose the Percentage chart metric you can set the decimal precision (either 0 or 1) for the data as per your choice. Minimum response count value - Using this setting you can add the minimum response count condition. Once the restriction is applied system will not show the data for countries which does not have the responses count more than the set condition. Please note that the value entered here is in terms of response count and not response percentage. Zoom in and out - You can zoom in and out using the provided + and - buttons on the top left corner of the widget. Using this you can zoom in and drag on the countries which you want to see data for. Once you click on any country, in tooltip system will show the name of the country along with other details such as response count or response shares. Note: As the metadata collected has information for country level only, the map chart shows data for countries only and is restricted to show state-level data. Was the article helpful?
